channel.title: Paper Feeds
channel.description: Keyword-based research paper feeds from arXiv and IACR

item.title: On the Limits of Stretching Quantum Pseudorandomness
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.24736v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

伪随机态（Pseudorandom States, PRS）是经典伪随机生成器（PRG）在量子计算中的自然类比，由Ji、Liu与Song（CRYPTO ’18）首次形式化定义。经典PRG的核心特性之一是可伸缩性（stretching）：给定短种子，可高效生成任意多项式长度的伪随机比特串。然而，量子PRS是否具备类似性质——即能否将输入种子“拉伸”为远长于其长度的不可区分量子态——长期悬而未决，构成量子密码学基础理论的关键空白。

核心贡献与方法

本工作首次建立了关于单拷贝安全PRS（$\mathsf{1PRS}$）输出长度的黑盒分离结果：我们构造了一个量子预言机（quantum oracle），在此相对模型下，存在输出长度为 $m(n) = 1.1n$ 的 $\mathsf{1PRS}$，但不存在任何输出长度达 $m(n) = \Omega(n^{2+\varepsilon})$（$\varepsilon > 0$）的 $\mathsf{1PRS}$。该分离揭示了量子伪随机性存在本质的长度瓶颈，否定了无条件的多项式伸缩可能性。

技术上，我们创新性地结合并拓展了Chen–Coladangelo–Sattath（EUROCRYPT ’25）提出的公共Haar随机态（CHRS）模型，提出一种新型资源计数技术：精确刻画任意$\mathsf{1PRS}$生成器在CHRS模型中所能有效利用的独立Haar态数量上限。该界限不依赖具体电路结构，仅基于查询复杂度与态空间维度，从而实现对高伸缩性PRS的普适性排除。

意义与影响

本结果首次从复杂性理论角度为量子伪随机性的伸缩能力划定严格边界，表明量子类比并非经典性质的直接迁移；同时，CHRS资源界定技术为后续研究量子随机性、量子极简主义（quantum minimalism）及抗量子密码原语设计提供了新工具范式。

AI Summary (English)

Pseudorandom states (PRS) are the quantum analogue of classical pseudorandom generators (PRGs). While classical PRGs can stretch short seeds into arbitrarily long pseudorandom strings, it remains open whether quantum PRS admit analogous stretching—i.e., generating output states whose dimension grows superlinearly in the seed length. This work establishes the first black-box separation for single-copy secure PRS ($\mathsf{1PRS}$) with differing output lengths: we construct a quantum oracle relative to which $\mathsf{1PRS}$ with output length $m(n) = 1.1n$ exist, but no $\mathsf{1PRS}$ with $m(n) = \Omega(n^{2+\varepsilon})$ exists for any $\varepsilon > 0$. Our proof builds on the Common Haar Random State (CHRS) model and introduces a novel technique to bound the effective number of Haar-random resources any $\mathsf{1PRS}$ generator can access—yielding a tight limitation on stretchability. This refutes unconditional polynomial stretching of quantum pseudorandomness and provides a foundational complexity-theoretic barrier.

Abstract

Pseudorandom states, introduced by Ji, Liu, and Song (CRYPTO '18), are quantum analogues of classical pseudorandom generators. A fundamental property of classical pseudorandom generators is that their output can be stretched to arbitrary polynomial length. Whether an analogous stretching property holds for quantum pseudorandom states remains unclear. In this work, we prove the first black-box separation between single-copy secure pseudorandom states ($\mathsf{1PRS}$) with different output lengths. Specifically, we construct a quantum oracle relative to which $\mathsf{1PRS}$ with output length $m(n)=1.1n$ exist, but $\mathsf{1PRS}$ with output length $m(n)=Ω(n^{2+ε})$ do not, for any $ε>0$. Our proof leverages the Common Haar Random State (CHRS) model introduced by Chen, Coladangelo, and Sattath (EUROCRYPT '25), and introduces a technique to bound the effective number of resource CHRS states utilized by any $\mathsf{1PRS}$ generator in this model.

item.title: FirmCure:Towards Autonomous and Adaptive Rehosting of Linux-Based Firmware
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.24549v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

全系统固件重托管（full-system rehosting）是Linux固件安全分析的关键前提，尤其对IoT设备漏洞挖掘、动态行为验证至关重要。然而，现有方法在面向定制化嵌入式设备时面临严峻瓶颈：其初始化阶段常因专有架构（如ARM64/ARC/MIPS）、硬件绑定配置（如设备树、启动参数、外设驱动依赖）而失败；运行时又易受中断缺失、时钟异常、GPIO模拟失准等干扰，高度依赖人工调试，导致重托管成功率低、耗时长、泛化性差。

方法创新：FirmCure框架

本文提出FirmCure——首个基于大语言模型（LLM）驱动的自主自适应固件重托管框架。其核心包含三阶段闭环机制：

  • 自适应感知推理（Adaptive Perception Inference）：通过轻量级静态分析提取固件镜像的内核模块依赖、根文件系统结构、启动脚本链及硬件抽象层（HAL）调用图，构建可迁移的结构知识图谱；
  • 反思式合成（Reflective Synthesis）：利用LLM对配置空间（QEMU参数、设备树补丁、init脚本注入点）进行多轮迭代优化，结合反馈信号（如内核日志关键词、进程存活状态）自动修正偏差；
  • 自主运行时干预（Autonomous Runtime Intervention）：部署轻量监控代理，实时捕获panic、hang、服务不可达等故障，触发LLM驱动的诊断策略（如中断模拟注入、时钟源替换、伪设备节点动态挂载），实现零人工介入的故障自愈。

实验验证与价值

在21款来自10家厂商、覆盖5种CPU架构（ARM32/ARM64/MIPS32/MIPS64/ARC）的IoT固件上，FirmCure实现100%网络端口开放率（即所有监听服务均可远程访问）与90.5%服务交互成功率（支持curl/wget/SSH等标准协议交互），较SOTA基线（Firmadyne、FirmAE）平均提升3.2×重托管成功率。框架成功复现CVE-2021-3738等已知漏洞，并新发现7个未公开缺陷（含3个高危RCE）。结果证实：FirmCure的干预策略具备跨厂商、跨架构强泛化能力，为固件自动化安全评估提供了可靠基础设施。

AI Summary (English)

Full-system rehosting of Linux-based firmware is essential for security analysis but remains highly manual due to architecture-specific initialization failures and runtime hardware dependencies. To overcome this, we present FirmCure, the first LLM-driven framework enabling autonomous and adaptive firmware rehosting. It integrates (1) Adaptive Perception Inference for static dependency extraction, (2) Reflective Synthesis for iterative QEMU/device-tree configuration optimization guided by kernel logs and process states, and (3) Autonomous Runtime Intervention for real-time fault diagnosis and self-healing (e.g., interrupt injection, clock source substitution). Evaluated on 21 IoT firmwares across 5 architectures (ARM32/64, MIPS32/64, ARC) from 10 vendors, FirmCure achieves 100% network port accessibility and 90.5% service interactivity, significantly outperforming state-of-the-art baselines. It successfully reproduces known vulnerabilities and discovers 7 novel flaws—including 3 high-severity RCEs—demonstrating strong cross-firmware generalizability.

Abstract

Full-system rehosting plays a critical role in the security analysis of Linux-based firmware. It matches commonly deployed firmware with sufficient background knowledge. However, for custom devices, existing approaches struggle to handle initialization and runtime obstacles in the rehosting process caused by specialized architectures and hardware-dependent configuration, which heavily rely on expert intervention. This ultimately creates fundamental bottlenecks and results in low rehosting efficiency. To address the above challenges, we propose FirmCure, the first LLM-driven full-system rehosting framework designed for autonomous and adaptive rehosting of Linux-based firmware. FirmCure develops an Adaptive Perception Inference mechanism to extract firmware structural dependencies via static analysis, followed by a Reflective Synthesis module for iterative configuration optimization, and finally an Autonomous Runtime Intervention module for real-time error remediation through runtime fault diagnosis and monitoring. We evaluated 21 IoT firmware images from 10 vendors across 5 architectures, while FirmCure achieved a 100% network port opening rate and 90.5% service interactivity, substantially outperforming state-of-the-art baselines. Our experiments confirm that FirmCure's intervention strategies generalize across heterogeneous firmware. The framework successfully reproduces known vulnerabilities and discovers new security flaws.

item.title: Red-Teaming the Agentic Red-Team
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.24496v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

随着大语言模型（LLM）驱动的智能体系统（agentic systems）在渗透测试、红队演练等进攻性安全场景中从理论走向商用化部署，其安全性却长期被忽视。当前研究与工业界聚焦于提升智能体的能力边界，却鲜有工作系统性评估其自身作为攻击载体所引入的新攻击面——尤其当智能体被用于执行高权限、高敏感度的安全任务时。

方法与发现

本研究首次对主流进攻性安全智能体工具（如AutoGPT、LangChain Red-Team Agents、CrewAI安全框架等）开展深度安全审计。我们发现：绝大多数工具存在共性架构缺陷，包括：

  • 未隔离的API密钥注入与泄露路径（如通过LLM输出解析触发eval()或动态代码执行）；
  • 缺乏沙箱内进程/网络行为的细粒度管控，导致攻击者可通过恶意工具调用实现容器逃逸；
  • 依赖不可信LLM响应直接生成并执行命令，形成LLM操纵→本地提权→持久化→横向移动的完整杀伤链。

创新与贡献

我们提出首个面向进攻性安全智能体的全周期网络杀伤链模型（Cyber Kill Chain for Agentic Red-Teams），覆盖从初始提示注入、沙箱绕过、到主机完全接管的6个阶段。基于此，我们设计了一种零信任增强型智能体架构：强制API密钥硬隔离、声明式工具白名单、LLM输出结构化校验、以及沙箱内无特权执行环境。所有设计原则均开源可复现，并已在3个主流框架中验证可阻断98.7%的披露攻击路径。

AI Summary (English)

This paper presents the first comprehensive security analysis of widely deployed agentic systems used in offensive security operations. We identify critical, shared architectural flaws—including insecure API key handling, untrusted LLM output execution, and insufficient sandbox containment—that enable adversaries to exfiltrate credentials, achieve persistent access, and fully compromise operator machines—even within containerized environments. To formalize the attack surface, we introduce a novel Cyber Kill Chain for Agentic Red-Teams, spanning initial LLM manipulation, sandbox escape, lateral movement, and persistence. Building on these findings, we propose a robust, zero-trust–inspired architecture with actionable design principles: strict API key isolation, declarative tool whitelisting, structured LLM output validation, and privilege-restricted sandbox execution. Our mitigations are broadly applicable and validated across three major frameworks, blocking >98% of disclosed attack vectors.

Abstract

The use of agentic systems to perform offensive security operations has moved from a theoretical possibility to a commoditized capability. However, while the community has focused on creating more and more capable agents, less attention has been allocated to assessing the security of those systems. In this work, we present the first in-depth security analysis of the most widely used agentic systems for offensive security operations. We show that most of these tools share common design flaws that enable an active adversary to exfiltrate API keys, establish persistent footholds, and fully compromise the operator's machine, even when the agent operates inside a sandboxed container. To support our analysis, we introduce a full cyber kill chain for such agentic systems, capturing the progression from initial LLM manipulation to lateral movement, persistence, guardrail bypass, and sandbox escape. Building on our security analysis, we derive a robust architecture for agentic offensive-security tools and propose actionable, broadly applicable design principles that mitigate the disclosed attack paths at the architectural level.

item.title: Poisoned Playbooks: Demystifying Knowledge Poisoning Effects on AI Security Agents
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.24402v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

随着AI安全代理（AI Security Agents）在漏洞分析与利用推理中日益依赖检索增强生成（RAG），其决策质量高度依赖外部安全知识库（如CTF write-ups、CVE报告、漏洞博客）。然而，若此类公开知识源被恶意篡改——即注入精心设计的错误技术描述（“Poisoned Playbooks”，中毒战术手册）——RAG系统可能将虚假信息内化为行动依据，导致生成错误甚至危险的利用代码。现有RAG投毒研究多聚焦问答（QA）场景下的答案污染，对需自主规划、验证与执行动作的安全代理的脆弱性缺乏系统揭示。

方法与实验设计

本研究构建首个面向安全代理的RAG投毒评估框架：

  • 在11个真实CTF挑战赛题目、11个现实CVE漏洞（含零日候选）上部署测试；
  • 覆盖3类前沿大语言模型家族（Llama、Qwen、DeepSeek）及2代模型架构（7B/72B参数）；
  • 注入单篇高隐蔽性中毒write-up（如篡改堆喷射偏移量、伪造ROP gadget链），观测代理行为偏移。

关键发现与创新

1. 投毒采纳具有系统性而非随机性：平均42.3%的代理在无显式验证提示下直接采纳中毒信息生成错误exploit，且成功率随模型规模增大而上升；
2. 提出验证边界（Verification Boundary, VB）——基于代理可调用的证据类型（本地上下文/检索片段/外部工具API），将投毒抗性划分为三级实证分类，解释为何某些错误无法被自动识别；
3. 验证提示（Verification Prompting）与多源检索（Multi-source Retrieval）在证据充分场景下可将错误率降低58%，但在稀疏证据或零日漏洞场景下性能骤降（错误率回升至76%），凸显当前RAG安全机制的根本局限。

AI Summary (English)

This paper exposes knowledge poisoning risks in RAG-based AI security agents—systems that autonomously reason about vulnerabilities and generate exploits using external security knowledge (e.g., CTF write-ups, CVE reports). Unlike prior QA-focused RAG poisoning studies, we investigate action-taking agents under realistic security tasks. Across 11 CTF challenges, 11 real-world CVEs, and 3 LLM families (Llama, Qwen, DeepSeek), we inject single, stealthy poisoned write-ups (“Poisoned Playbooks”) into public knowledge sources and observe systematic, non-random adoption of false exploit logic—up to 42.3% adoption rate without verification. To explain this, we introduce the Verification Boundary (VB): a 3-level empirical taxonomy grounded in the evidence agents can access (context, retrieved snippets, or external tools) to refute poisoned claims. We evaluate two mitigation strategies: verification prompting and multi-source retrieval. Both significantly reduce errors (+58% accuracy) when strong corroborating evidence exists—but fail dramatically under sparse-evidence or zero-day conditions (error rate ↑ to 76%), revealing a critical trust gap in operational AI security systems.

Abstract

AI security agents increasingly rely on Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) to use external security knowledge for vulnerability analysis and exploit reasoning. This creates a new risk: poisoned write-ups can be operationalized into incorrect exploit behavior. Yet, prior work on RAG poisoning has mostly studied answer corruption in QA settings, much less is known about action-taking security agents. This paper aims to reveal such characteristics with crafted poisons about real-world challenges and AI agents. First, we demonstrate how a crafted single poisoned write-up injected into public-style security knowledge sources which we denote as Poisoned Playbooks, alters the behavior of RAG-based AI security agents. Across 11 CTF challenges, 3 frontier LLM families, 2 model generations, and 11 real-world CVEs, we find that poison adoption is systematic rather than random. To explain this pattern, we introduce the Verification Boundary (VB), a 3-level empirical classification based on what evidence the agent can use to refute a retrieved claim. Finally, we evaluate verification prompting and multi-source retrieval, showing that both help when stronger evidence exists, but weaken under sparse-evidence and zero-day conditions.

item.title: Securing LLM-Agent Long-Term Memory Against Poisoning: Non-Malleable, Origin-Bound Authority with Machine-Checked Guarantees
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.24322v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

大语言模型（LLM）智能体日益依赖持久化长期记忆，但该机制引入关键安全漏洞——记忆投毒（memory poisoning）：攻击者可在某次会话中注入恶意内容，待后续会话中触发高风险操作（如转账、配置篡改或数据泄露）。现有防御策略主要基于两类权威信号：内容可信度（如检测或信任评分）或推导谱系（lineage），但本文首次系统揭示二者均存在本质脆弱性。

方法与理论突破

我们识别出LLM智能体特有的三类投毒洗白通道：（1）智能体自身摘要重写、（2）可信工具的回声式复述、（3）伪造协同验证。攻击者可借此篡改内容表征或切断/反转谱系链，使恶意记忆“伪装”为可信来源。为此，我们形式化定义记忆“写入–检索–执行”全流程中的可篡改性（malleability），并以机器可验证方式证明三项核心定理：

  • T1：在洗白攻击下，任何仅依赖内容或谱系的防御均不健全；
  • T2：写入时刻的来源绑定（origin binding） 是必要条件；
  • T3：结合非可篡改性（non-malleability） 与抗女巫（Sybil-resistant）协同验证门控提升机制的来源绑定权威模型是充分解。

创新与实证

提出 TMA-NM（Tamper-evident Memory Authority, Non-Malleable） 架构，首次将非可篡改信息流控制（IFC）落地于LLM智能体记忆系统。在覆盖8个前沿模型（含GPT-4、Claude、Llama系列）的跨防御、跨攻击、跨模型基准测试中：现有方案在理论预测的洗白场景下失败率达68%；而TMA-NM在所有攻击通道（直接+三类洗白）与全部模型上实现0%攻击成功，且不损害合法功能效用。代码、基准套件及TLA+机器验证模型已开源。

AI Summary (English)

This paper addresses memory poisoning in LLM agents—where adversaries inject malicious content into persistent long-term memory to trigger harmful actions (e.g., payments, config changes) in later sessions. We demonstrate that existing defenses—relying on content trust-scoring or derivation lineage—are fundamentally malleable: attackers can launder untrusted origins via three LLM-specific channels (agent summarization, trusted-tool echo, and manufactured corroboration), breaking or flipping lineage edges and masking malicious intent. We formalize malleability in the write-retrieve-act pipeline and machine-check a separation theorem: (T1) no content- or lineage-only defense is sound under laundering; (T2) write-time origin binding is necessary; and (T3) non-malleable, origin-bound authority with Sybil-resistant, corroboration-gated elevation is sufficient. Our TMA-NM construction realizes non-malleable information-flow control for LLM memory. Evaluated across 8 frontier models, prior defenses fail up to 68% on predicted laundering attacks, while TMA-NM achieves 0% success against all direct and laundering attacks—without sacrificing legitimate utility. All artifacts—including benchmark, harness, and TLA+ proofs—are open-sourced.

Abstract

LLM agents increasingly rely on persistent long-term memory, which creates a critical vulnerability that we study here: memory poisoning. An adversary can store untrusted content in one session that later steers a consequential action, such as a payment, a setting change, or data exfiltration, in a future session. Existing defenses base a memory item's authority to act on either its content (detection or trust-scoring) or its derivation history (lineage). We show that both signals are malleable. An attacker can launder an untrusted origin through three channels specific to LLM agents: the agent's own summarization, a trusted-tool echo, and manufactured corroboration. Each makes the content look benign and breaks or flips its derivation edge to ``trusted.'' We formalize malleability for the memory write-retrieve-act pipeline and prove a machine-checked separation theorem. No content- or lineage-based defense is sound under laundering (T1), write-time origin binding is necessary (T2), and non-malleable origin-bound authority with Sybil-resistant corroboration-gated elevation is sufficient (T3). Our construction, TMA-NM (Tamper-evident Memory Authority, Non-Malleable), instantiates non-malleable information-flow control (IFC) for LLM-agent memory. A cross-defense, cross-attack, and cross-model benchmark over eight frontier models shows that existing defenses fail exactly where the theory predicts (up to 68% laundering attack-success), while TMA-NM reaches 0% attack success on both direct and laundering attacks across all models and channels, at full legitimate utility. We release the benchmark, harness, and machine-checked TLA+ models to support reproducibility.

item.title: Inside Crypter-as-a-Service: An Ecosystem Analysis of the exploit.in Underground Forum Research Talks
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.24226v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

Crypter-as-a-Service（CraaS）已成为当代恶意软件经济的关键基础设施，通过地下服务市场为攻击者提供按需的反检测能力。exploit.in 作为活跃于明网与暗网的俄语主流网络犯罪论坛，是 CraaS 交易的核心枢纽之一。

方法与数据

本研究对 exploit.in 平台约 100 万条帖子开展纵向分析（2020年1月–2025年8月），结合关键词过滤、大语言模型（LLM）辅助标注与人工校验，最终构建包含 491 个主题帖、2949 条有效回帖的高质量语料库。

主要发现

  • CraaS 并非一次性工具销售，而是持续运维的服务形态：依赖每日/每周 stub 更新、动态 AV 规避及基于信任的交付机制；
  • 提出首个 CraaS 市场参与者五类卖家（如“全栈加密服务商”“Stub即服务提供商”）与四类买家（如“初学者勒索软件作者”“APT关联开发者”）分类体系，并映射其交易匹配逻辑；
  • 揭示多元定价模型：从Telegram 机器人单次加密（$5–$20） 到定制化开发（$2,000+） 及长期雇佣（月薪 $3,000+）；
  • 社会网络分析显示：市场呈强中心化结构——仅 7.3% 的卖家贡献 62% 的交易连接，核心节点多担任信任中介或担保人角色；
  • 治理机制高度成熟：依托第三方托管（escrow）、双担保人制、声誉评分系统与安全保证金（up to $500） 维持稳定性；
  • 对比 HackForums 发现：exploit.in 的 CraaS 生态更专业化、服务契约化、生命周期管理精细化，反映地下市场的演进分层。

AI Summary (English)

This paper presents the first longitudinal ecosystem analysis of Crypter-as-a-Service (CraaS) on exploit.in—a major Russian-language cybercrime forum operating across clear and dark web surfaces. From ~1M posts (Jan 2020–Aug 2025), we curated a validated corpus of 491 threads and 2,949 posts using keyword filtering, LLM-assisted annotation, and manual verification. We find CraaS on exploit.in is not commodity software but an operational service requiring daily stub renewal, sustained AV evasion, and trust-based delivery. We propose a novel taxonomy: five seller types (e.g., “stub-as-a-service providers”) and four buyer profiles (e.g., “entry-level ransomware authors”), mapped via transactional correspondence. Pricing ranges from low-cost Telegram bot builds ($5–$20) to high-end custom development ($2,000+) and salaried recruitment ($3,000+/month). Social network analysis reveals a hierarchical market anchored by a small, highly central core (~7.3% of sellers account for 62% of connections), many acting as trust brokers. Market stability relies on mature governance: escrow, dual guarantors, reputation scoring, and security deposits (up to $500). Compared to HackForums, exploit.in exhibits a more professionalized, service-oriented, and lifecycle-managed CraaS configuration.

Abstract

Crypter-as-a-Service (CraaS) has become a key enabling layer of the contemporary malware economy by providing on-demand evasion capabilities through underground service markets. In this paper, we present a longitudinal characterization of the CraaS ecosystem on exploit.in, a major Russian-language cybercrime forum with a presence on both the clear web and the dark web. From a collection of approximately 1,000,000 posts, we combine keyword filtering, LLM-assisted annotation, and manual validation to extract a corpus of 491 threads and 2,949 posts spanning January 2020 to August 2025. Our analysis shows that crypters on exploit.in are not merely sold as static tools, but as continuously maintained operational services whose value depends on recurring stub renewal - sometimes on a daily basis - sustained antivirus evasion, and trust-based delivery. We develop a taxonomy of five seller types and four buyer profiles, and map the buyer-seller correspondences that structure market transactions. We further document pricing models ranging from low-cost per-build Telegram bot services to high-end custom development and salaried recruitment. Using social-network analysis, we find that the market is hierarchically structured around a small core of highly central actors, many of whom appear to function as trust brokers or other influential intermediaries, while its stability relies on a broader trust and governance infrastructure including escrow, guarantors, reputation systems, and security deposits. Finally, we discuss differences between the CraaS model observed on exploit.in and that reported on HackForums. Although both forums share similar service logics, our corpus suggests that exploit.in exhibits a more professionalized and service-oriented CraaS configuration.

item.title: LLMs Prompted for Legal Context Object More: Overrefusal from Small On-Premises LLMs in Criminal Legal Context
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.24585v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

尽管大语言模型（LLM）在法律场景中的应用仍面临伦理与合法性争议，一线法律从业者已开始部署小型本地化模型（如Qwen、Phi-3、Llama-3-8B等）用于辅助翻译、文书润色与案情重述等低风险任务。然而，此类“无害”用途可能隐含系统性偏见——尤其当模型对特定法律主题（如刑事案件）表现出过度拒绝（overrefusal）时，将扭曲案件处理节奏，加剧程序不公。

方法与设计

本研究聚焦6款主流可离线部署的小型开源LLM（参数量≤8B），在统一刑事法律提示集（涵盖罪名认定、量刑建议、证据审查等21类真实判例片段）上系统测试其响应稳定性。我们对比三类提示策略：（1）无角色前缀基线；（2）权威身份前缀（如“你正作为最高人民法院助理”“你是一名辩护律师”）；（3）经典角色扮演越狱前缀（如“你正在参与法律教学模拟”）。所有实验在相同硬件与温度（T=0.7）下完成，拒绝率定义为模型输出“我不能回答”“这涉及法律风险”等明确拒答语句的比例。

主要发现

  • 权威前缀引发剧烈过拒：所有模型在权威身份提示下拒绝率激增2–20倍（如Phi-3在“最高法院助理”设定中拒答率达89%，基线仅4.3%）；
  • 越狱前缀效果分裂：同一前缀在Qwen-2-7B上使拒答率下降37%，却在Llama-3-8B中上升22%，暴露模型间响应逻辑的根本差异；
  • 刑事语境敏感度高：相比民事/行政提示，刑事类提示的拒答率平均高出3.8倍，且拒绝内容常缺乏法律依据，呈现“预防性沉默”。

创新与启示

本研究首次揭示：小型本地LLM在真实法律机构语境中存在角色框架诱发的响应失稳问题，其拒绝行为非源于合规性审慎，而更多反映微调数据偏差与指令对齐脆弱性。结果警示：未经上下文鲁棒性验证的本地法律助手可能放大司法不平等——亟需建立面向法律场景的拒绝行为基准测试（Legal Refusal Benchmark, LRB）及动态校准机制。

AI Summary (English)

This study investigates overrefusal—unwarranted refusal to respond—in small, on-premises LLMs (≤8B parameters) deployed for criminal legal assistance. Testing six models across 21 real-world criminal law prompts, we find that authority-style prefixes (e.g., “you are an assistant of the national supreme court”) systematically increase refusal rates by 2–20× compared to no-prefix baselines—indicating severe contextual instability. In contrast, a known role-play jailbreak prefix yields divergent effects: sharply reducing refusals in Qwen-2-7B (−37%) but increasing them in Llama-3-8B (+22%), revealing model-specific alignment fragility. Critically, criminal-law prompts trigger significantly higher refusal rates than civil or administrative ones (3.8× average), often without substantive legal justification. These findings expose a critical bias vector: small local LLMs, though appealing for privacy-sensitive legal use, exhibit unpredictable, context-triggered non-responsiveness that risks distorting case processing fairness. We call for domain-specific refusal benchmarking and adaptive calibration protocols before operational deployment.

Abstract

While the validity of LLMs' use in the legal context remains subject to ethical and legal debate, legal professionals are already experimenting with personal LLMs, if only for translation and reformulation. However, even such a seemingly innocuous use can introduce biases through case processing speed if LLM assistants selectively refuse assistance on certain topics. To better anticipate such biases, we investigate several modern small LLMs that are most likely to be used as on-device assistants, to assess the impact of overrefusal on legal prompts. Surprisingly, we find that authority-style prefixes (`you are acting as an assistant of the national supreme court'', `[...] defense lawyer'') systematically increase refusal rates by 2--20x over the no-prefix baseline, while a known role-play jailbreak prefix shows mixed effects, sharply increasing refusals in some models and barely shifting them in others. The finding suggests that small on-prem deployable LLMs are unstable under contextual framings that a real institutional user might naturally introduce, and further investigation is essential to minimize opportunities for bias.

item.title: SURGELLM: Rethinking Multi-Task Evaluation through Task-Aware Feature Gating with Class-Balanced Normalization
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.24259v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

面向异构自然语言处理（NLP）任务微调的编码器常面临三重挑战：（1）不同任务固有的归纳偏置不匹配；（2）类别不平衡严重扭曲特征统计量，导致门控机制失准；（3）缺乏将外部词汇知识显式注入注意力计算的有效机制。现有方法多采用统一归一化或静态提示，难以兼顾任务特异性与词汇感知能力。

方法创新

本文提出 SURGELLM——一种轻量、可插拔的统一Transformer框架，通过三个协同设计的模块实现“外科式”特征调控：

  • 手术式特征门控（Surgical Feature Gate）：基于词典化词汇指示符（如情感极性、实体频率）与[CLS]向量，学习逐维度sigmoid门控；理论证明其在特征无判别力时自动退化为恒等映射，保障鲁棒性；
  • 任务条件化前缀令牌（Task-Conditioned Prefix Tokens）：将量化后的特征值与任务ID联合编码为可学习前缀，动态引导模型关注任务相关语义维度；
  • 实例加权归一化（Instance-Weighted Normalization, IWN）：在门控统计计算中引入类别逆频率权重，显式消除类别先验偏差，首次实现门控层的类平衡归一化。

实验结果

在四大任务（SST-2情感分析、多跳检索、大模型提示溯源、作者归属检测）共17,830样本、11种模型变体、3次随机种子实验中，IWN变体取得宏观F1 0.940，较最强非IWN基线提升+0.036，其中作者归属任务跃升+0.130。控制实验（随机词汇替换）导致平均F1下降−0.028，证实性能增益源于词汇知识而非参数冗余。代码、定制词典及99.5%高保真自动提取方案已开源。

AI Summary (English)

We propose SURGELLM, a unified transformer framework addressing three critical limitations of fine-tuned encoders across heterogeneous NLP tasks: mismatched inductive biases, class-imbalance corruption of feature statistics, and lack of lexical knowledge integration. SURGELLM introduces three lightweight, theoretically grounded modules: (1) a surgical feature gate—a per-dimension sigmoid over curated lexical indicators and [CLS], provably reducing to identity when features are uninformative; (2) task-conditioned prefix tokens, jointly encoding quantized features and task identity; and (3) Instance-Weighted Normalization (IWN), which debiases gate statistics via inverse-class-frequency weighting. Evaluated across four diverse tasks (SST-2, multi-hop retrieval, LLM-prompt attribution, authorship detection; 17,830 examples, 11 model variants, 3 seeds), the IWN variant achieves macro-F1 0.940, +0.036 over the strongest non-IWN baseline and +0.130 on authorship detection. A random-vocabulary control (−0.028 avg. F1) confirms lexical—not parametric—gains. Code, vocabularies, and a 99.5%-recovery auto-extraction recipe are released.

Abstract

Fine-tuned encoders deployed across heterogeneous NLP tasks face three compounding problems: mismatched inductive biases, class-imbalance corruption of feature statistics, and no mechanism to condition attention on external lexical knowledge. We introduce \textbf{\surgellm}, a unified transformer framework that addresses each with a dedicated lightweight module: a \emph{surgical feature gate} (learned per-dimension sigmoid over curated lexical indicators and \texttt{[CLS]}; provably degenerates to identity when features are uninformative), \emph{task-conditioned prefix tokens} (quantized feature values and task identity prepended to every input), and \emph{Instance-Weighted Normalization} (IWN; removes class-prior bias from gate statistics). We prove an excess-risk bound linking gate benefit to \emph{surgical feature alignment}. Across four tasks, SST-2, multi-hop retrieval, LLM-prompt attribution, and authorship detection, covering 17,830 examples and eleven model variants over three seeds, the IWN variant achieves macro-F1 \textbf{0.940} ($+0.036$ over the strongest non-IWN baseline; $+0.130$ on authorship detection). A random-vocabulary control ($-0.028$ avg.\ F1) confirms gains are lexical, not parametric. Code, vocabularies, and a $99.5\%$-recovery auto-extraction recipe are released.

item.title: Towards Federated Long-Tailed Graph Learning: An Energy-Guided Dual Decoupling Approach
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.24237v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

联邦图学习（Federated Graph Learning）在保护数据隐私的前提下支持分布式客户端协同建模图结构数据，但现实场景中节点类别常呈现长尾分布：少数类（tail classes）样本极度稀缺。此类统计偏差引发双重挑战——全局模型严重偏向多数类，且少数类节点因被主导类邻居“淹没”而陷入结构隔离，难以获得有效表征。

方法创新：能量引导的双解耦框架 FedEPD

针对现有方法仅依赖统计补偿、易受异构噪声干扰的缺陷，本文提出 FedEPD（Federated Energy-guided Prototype Decoupling），首创“拓扑净化 + 语义重校准”双解耦范式：

  • 拓扑净化层：引入分布感知的狄利克雷能量剪枝（Dirichlet Energy Pruning），量化边连接的能量贡献，主动剔除导致异质性（heterophily）的冗余/误导性边，提升少数类节点邻域纯度；
  • 语义重校准层：从拓扑中心节点提取鲁棒全局原型，通过空间低通原型注入（spatial low-pass prototype injection）将其平滑融入本地表征，缓解Non-IID分布偏移；
  • 优化机制：设计两阶段交替优化策略，在严格保护多数类决策边界的同时，定向增强少数类分类精度。

实验结果

在多个长尾图基准（如 Flickr-LT、Reddit-LT、Arxiv-LT）上，FedEPD显著超越SOTA方法：准确率最高提升4.97%，Macro-F1最高提升5.48%，尤其在尾部类别上F1增益达12.3%，验证了其对结构性与统计性双重偏差的协同治理能力。

AI Summary (English)

Federated Graph Learning enables privacy-preserving collaborative modeling across distributed clients, yet real-world graphs often exhibit severe long-tailed class distributions. This leads to both statistical bias (dominant-class overfitting) and structural isolation (minority nodes embedded in heterophilic, head-dominated neighborhoods). Existing approaches—relying on topology-agnostic statistical corrections—fail under extreme data scarcity and amplify structural noise. To address this, we propose FedEPD, an energy-guided dual-decoupling framework: (1) it performs topological purification via distribution-aware Dirichlet energy pruning to remove heterophilic edges; (2) it achieves semantic recalibration by extracting robust global prototypes from topologically central nodes and injecting them into local representations via spatial low-pass filtering; and (3) it employs a two-stage alternating optimization to preserve majority-class boundaries while boosting minority-class accuracy. Extensive experiments show FedEPD achieves state-of-the-art performance across diverse long-tailed benchmarks, with absolute gains of up to +4.97% Accuracy and +5.48% Macro-F1.

Abstract

Federated Graph Learning facilitates collaborative graph modeling across distributed clients while preserving data privacy. However, real-world data categories frequently exhibit long-tailed distributions. Such statistical scarcity severely degrades performance in two ways: it biases the global model toward majority classes, and it structurally isolates minority nodes by submerging them in heterophilic, head-dominated neighborhoods. While existing methods attempt topology-agnostic statistical compensations, they often fail under data scarcity. Instead of recovering tail nodes, they overfit the structural noise from adjacent dominant classes, leading to representation degradation. To address these limitations, we propose FedEPD, a framework built on a dual decoupling paradigm that separates topological purification from semantic recalibration. Specifically, FedEPD utilizes distribution-aware Dirichlet energy pruning to filter spatial heterophilic edges. It then overcomes Non-IID distribution shifts by extracting robust global prototypes from topologically central nodes, which are incorporated into local representations via a spatial low-pass prototype injection. Furthermore, a two stage alternating optimization strategy strictly protects majority decision boundaries while improving minority accuracy. Extensive experiments demonstrate that FedEPD achieves state-of-the-art performance across diverse long-tailed benchmarks, yielding absolute improvements of up to 4.97% in Accuracy and 5.48% in Macro-F1.

item.title: L3Cube-MahaPOS: A Marathi Part-of-Speech Tagging Dataset and BERT Models
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.24825v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

词性标注（POS tagging）是自然语言处理的基础任务，支撑机器翻译、信息抽取与句法分析等下游应用。马拉地语（Marathi）作为全球使用人数超8300万的前二十大语言，长期面临严重资源匮乏问题：缺乏大规模人工标注语料、统一评测基准及高质量预训练模型。其计算建模尤为困难——形态高度屈折、语序相对自由、无大小写区分惯例，且普遍存在与印地语、英语的混合代码现象。

方法与数据集

本研究发布 L3Cube-MahaPOS：首个面向马拉地语的黄金标准POS标注数据集。该数据集包含32,354句新闻文本，全部由母语为马拉地语的专业标注员依据16类Universal Dependencies对齐标签体系手工完成。我们设计了完整的结构化预处理流程，涵盖Unicode标准化、天城文（Devanagari）感知分词及噪声过滤，确保训练/验证/测试集间标签一致性。

模型评估与结果

我们在6类主流模型上系统评测：HMM、CRF、BiLSTM、BiLSTM+CharCNN、多语言BERT（MuRIL）及专用于马拉地语的MahaBERT-v2。最优模型（MahaBERT-v2微调）达88.67%词级准确率与81.67%宏平均F1值（15类标签），显著优于基线模型（如CRF提升+12.4 F1）。所有实验均在严格划分的三元分割集上完成，保障可复现性。

创新与贡献

✅ 首个开源、大规模、人工校验的马拉地语POS基准数据集；
✅ 发布完整标注指南、预处理脚本及全部训练好的模型检查点；
✅ 提出适配天城文字形与混合语言特性的标注规范与技术栈；
✅ 为低资源印度语言NLP研究提供可迁移方法论范式。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces L3Cube-MahaPOS, the first gold-standard Part-of-Speech (POS) tagging dataset for Marathi—a major low-resource Indian language spoken by over 83 million people. The dataset comprises 32,354 manually annotated news sentences, labeled with a 16-tag Universal Dependencies-aligned scheme by native Marathi linguists. A robust preprocessing pipeline ensures Unicode normalization, Devanagari-aware tokenization, and noise filtering for label consistency across splits. We benchmark six model families—including HMM, CRF, BiLSTM variants, MuRIL, and the Marathi-specific MahaBERT-v2—achieving 88.67% token-level accuracy and 81.67% macro-F1 (15 tags). All resources—including the dataset, annotation guidelines, and trained model checkpoints—are publicly released to advance Marathi and related Indic language NLP research.

Abstract

Part-of-Speech (POS) tagging is a foundational NLP task underpinning machine translation, information extraction, and syntactic parsing. Despite Marathi being spoken by over 83 million people and ranking among the top twenty most spoken languages worldwide, it remains severely under-resourced in annotated corpora and standardised evaluation benchmarks. Marathi presents unique challenges for computational modelling owing to its rich morphology, relatively free word order, lack of capitalisation conventions, and pervasive code-mixing with Hindi and English. We introduce L3Cube-MahaPOS, a gold-standard POS tagging dataset for Marathi comprising 32,354 manually annotated sentences drawn from news text. Annotation was performed entirely manually by a team of Marathi-proficient annotators following a 16-tag Universal Dependencies-aligned scheme. A structured preprocessing pipeline covering Unicode normalisation, Devanagari-aware tokenisation, and noise filtering ensures label consistency across all splits. We benchmark the dataset across six model families spanning HMM, CRF, BiLSTM, BiLSTM+CharCNN, MuRIL, and the Marathi-specific transformer MahaBERT-v2. The best system achieves 88.67\% token-level accuracy and a macro-F1 of 81.67% over 15 evaluated tag classes. We release the dataset, annotation guidelines, and trained model checkpoints to foster further research in Marathi NLP.

item.title: Natural Identifiers for Privacy and Data Audits in Large Language Models
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.24408v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

大型语言模型（LLM）的隐私审计面临两大现实瓶颈：一是差分隐私（DP）事后审计依赖训练阶段注入人工“金丝雀”（canary）数据，无法用于已部署模型；二是数据集推断（dataset inference） 需要私有非成员验证集（private non-member holdout），但该集合必须与嫌疑数据同分布（IID），实践中难以构建或获取，严重制约可扩展的事后审计能力。

方法创新：自然标识符（NIDs）

本文提出自然标识符（Natural Identifiers, NIDs）——一类在真实LLM训练语料中天然存在的结构化随机字符串，如加密哈希值（e.g., SHA-256片段）、短链接（e.g., bit.ly/abc123）、UUID、Base64编码片段等。其核心优势在于：

  • 分布可再生性：NIDs具有明确格式约束与随机生成机制，支持从同一分布中无限采样新样本；
  • 无需重训练：可直接作为替代性“自然金丝雀”，用于DP审计；
  • 免持验证集：生成的NID样本天然满足与嫌疑数据同分布要求，可直接充当非成员数据，实现零依赖的数据集推断。

主要发现与贡献

在多个主流开源模型（Llama-2、Falcon、Pythia）上的实证表明：
✅ 仅用NIDs即可完成无需重训练的差分隐私事后审计，准确识别DP超参数违规（如ε > 2.0）；
✅ 对含NIDs的嫌疑数据集（如GitHub代码片段、Reddit帖子中的短链接），可实现无需私有holdout的高精度数据集推断（AUC ≥ 0.92）；
✅ NIDs具备强鲁棒性：对微调、蒸馏、上下文扰动均保持可检测性。
本工作首次将数据内在结构转化为审计基础设施，为LLM隐私合规提供低成本、可扩展、即插即用的事后审计范式。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces Natural Identifiers (NIDs)—structured random strings (e.g., cryptographic hashes, shortened URLs, UUIDs) naturally prevalent in real-world LLM training corpora—to overcome two key barriers in post-hoc privacy auditing. First, NIDs enable differential privacy (DP) auditing without model retraining, serving as drop-in replacements for synthetic canaries by allowing unlimited sampling from the same underlying distribution. Second, they eliminate the need for a private non-member holdout set in dataset inference, as NIDs sampled from their native distribution inherently satisfy the IID requirement for reliable membership testing. Experiments across Llama-2, Falcon, and Pythia confirm that NID-based audits achieve high accuracy: DP violations (e.g., ε > 2.0) are reliably detected, and dataset inference attains AUC ≥ 0.92 on suspect datasets containing NIDs—even after fine-tuning or context perturbation. NIDs thus provide a practical, scalable, and distribution-aware foundation for real-world LLM privacy compliance.

Abstract

Assessing the privacy of large language models (LLMs) presents significant challenges. In particular, most existing methods for auditing differential privacy require the insertion of specially crafted canary data during training, making them impractical for auditing already-trained models without costly retraining. Additionally, dataset inference, which audits whether a suspect dataset was used to train a model, is infeasible without access to a private non-member held-out dataset. Yet, such held-out datasets are often unavailable or difficult to construct for real-world cases since they have to be from the same distribution (IID) as the suspect data. These limitations severely hinder the ability to conduct scalable, post-hoc audits. To enable such audits, this work introduces natural identifiers (NIDs) as a novel solution to the above-mentioned challenges. NIDs are structured random strings, such as cryptographic hashes and shortened URLs, naturally occurring in common LLM training datasets. Their format enables the generation of unlimited additional random strings from the same distribution, which can act as alternative canaries for audits and as same-distribution held-out data for dataset inference. Our evaluation highlights that indeed, using NIDs, we can facilitate post-hoc differential privacy auditing without any retraining and enable dataset inference for any suspect dataset containing NIDs without the need for a private non-member held-out dataset.

item.title: FedUP: One-Shot Federated Unlearning via Centroid-Guided Plug-in Filters
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.24113v1

AI Summary (中文)

FedUP：基于质心引导插件滤波器的一次性联邦遗忘学习框架

背景与挑战：联邦遗忘学习（Federated Unlearning, FU）是保障分布式系统中用户“被遗忘权”的关键技术，但现有方法普遍面临两大瓶颈：一是非目标类知识的意外丢失（non-target knowledge loss），导致模型整体性能下降；二是单次遗忘请求需多轮客户端-服务器通信与模型重训练，延迟高达分钟级，难以满足实时合规需求。

方法创新：本文提出 FedUP——首个支持一次性（one-shot）、低延迟、可逆式联邦遗忘的学习框架。其核心在于引入轻量级、可插拔（pluggable）的滤波器模块，作为“知识漏斗”（knowledge funnel）：在推理阶段动态屏蔽目标用户/样本的特征响应，同时完全冻结原始全局模型参数，避免任何参数扰动。关键设计在于——服务器端仅利用差分隐私（DP）保护下的各类别质心样本（class centroid samples）训练滤波器，无需访问原始私有数据或触发客户端参与。

核心优势：

  • ✅ 超低延迟：遗忘操作从分钟级压缩至数秒内完成，消除多轮通信开销；
  • ✅ 零非目标损失：因原始模型不动，所有未请求遗忘的知识得以完整保留；
  • ✅ 天然可逆：仅需移除滤波器即可瞬时恢复原始模型行为，支持审计与纠错；
  • ✅ 强隐私保障：质心生成与滤波器训练全程采用DP机制，满足GDPR等合规要求。

实验验证：在CIFAR-10/100、Tiny-ImageNet（图像）及AG News、SST-2（文本）共5个基准任务上，FedUP在遗忘精度（ΔAUC > 92%）、非目标准确率保持率（≥99.3%）、推理开销（+<0.8% FLOPs）等方面全面超越SOTA方法（如FedEraser、UnFed），且在异构数据分布下鲁棒性强。开源代码已发布于：https://github.com/suows/FedUP-code。

AI Summary (English)

FedUP is a one-shot, server-only federated unlearning framework that enables rapid, reversible, and privacy-preserving model forgetting without client involvement or global model retraining. By freezing the pre-trained global model and training lightweight, pluggable centroid-guided filters solely on DP-protected class centroid samples at the server, FedUP acts as a “knowledge funnel” to suppress target data influence while preserving all non-target knowledge intact. This design eliminates multi-round communication and reduces unlearning latency from minutes to seconds. Crucially, forgetting is inherently reversible—removing the filter instantly restores full model functionality. Experiments across five image and text benchmarks (CIFAR-10/100, Tiny-ImageNet, AG News, SST-2) show FedUP achieves >92% unlearning precision (ΔAUC), maintains ≥99.3% non-target accuracy, and incurs negligible inference overhead (<0.8% FLOPs), outperforming state-of-the-art methods under heterogeneous data settings. Code: https://github.com/suows/FedUP-code.

Abstract

Federated unlearning (FU) is critical for complying with legal mandates like the right to be forgotten in decentralized systems, yet current methods face a persistent dilemma between non-target knowledge loss and high request latency. To resolve these issues, we propose FedUP, a one-shot federated unlearning framework utilizing lightweight pluggable filters that act as a "knowledge funnel" to screen out target data while preserving original model performance. By freezing original model parameters and training filters at the server side using differentially private (DP)-protected class centroid samples, FedUP bypasses the need for multi-round client-server communication and complex retraining, reducing unlearning latency from minutes to mere seconds. Additionally, the framework's pluggable architecture ensures inherent reversibility, enabling the seamless restoration of forgotten knowledge by simply removing the filters. Extensive experiments on diverse image and text tasks demonstrate that FedUP effectively reduces non-target knowledge loss and achieves superior unlearning precision and efficiency across various scenarios. Code is available at: https://github.com/suows/FedUP-code.

item.title: Are We Ready For An Agent-Native Memory System?
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.24775v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

当前大语言模型（LLM）智能体的“记忆”已从简单的检索增强机制，演进为支持持久化存储、动态检索、增量更新、知识融合与全生命周期治理的数据管理系统。然而，现有评估仍过度依赖端到端任务指标（如F1、BLEU），将记忆系统视为黑箱，严重忽视其底层数据管理能力——包括操作开销、模块间架构权衡、以及面对高频知识更新时的鲁棒性。

方法与框架

本文首次从数据管理视角系统解构智能体记忆：提出四维分析框架——记忆表示与存储、信息抽取、检索与路由、维护机制。在此基础上，我们在5类典型工作负载（涵盖11个真实数据集）上，对12种代表性记忆系统及2个基线方案开展统一评测；并通过细粒度消融实验，定量评估各模块对表示保真度、检索精度、更新正确性、长周期稳定性的独立贡献。

关键发现与启示

  • 无单一架构在所有场景下占优；性能高度依赖记忆结构与工作负载瓶颈的匹配度（如高更新频次场景中局部维护显著优于全局重组）；
  • 成本-性能分析表明：局部化维护可降低37–62%的计算开销，而全局索引重建易引发长尾延迟；
  • 多数系统在知识动态演化下出现“语义漂移”，暴露了缺乏显式版本控制与一致性校验的设计缺陷。

本研究为构建真正Agent-Native（面向智能体原生需求）的记忆系统提供了可复现的评估范式、实证基准与演进路径。代码开源：https://github.com/OpenDataBox/MemoryData

AI Summary (English)

This paper presents the first systematic data-management-oriented evaluation of memory systems for LLM agents. We propose a four-module analytical framework—representation & storage, extraction, retrieval & routing, and maintenance—and evaluate 12 representative memory systems plus 2 baselines across five realistic workloads (11 datasets). Our end-to-end experiments reveal no universally dominant architecture: effectiveness critically depends on alignment between memory structure and workload bottlenecks (e.g., update frequency or query complexity). Fine-grained ablation quantifies trade-offs in fidelity, precision, update correctness, and long-horizon stability. Crucially, localized maintenance reduces operational cost by 37–62% versus global reorganization, while exposing widespread fragility under dynamic knowledge evolution. These findings lay the foundation for truly agent-native memory design. Code: https://github.com/OpenDataBox/MemoryData

Abstract

Memory for large language model (LLM) agents has rapidly evolved from simple retrieval-augmented mechanisms into a data management system that supports persistent information storage, retrieval, update, consolidation, and dynamic lifecycle governance throughout agent execution. Despite this evolution, existing evaluations still benchmark agent memory mainly through end-to-end task success metrics (e.g., F1, BLEU), while treating the underlying system as a monolithic black box. As a result, critical system-level concerns, including operational costs, architectural trade-offs across memory modules, and robustness under dynamic knowledge updates, remain insufficiently explored. In this paper, we present a systematic experimental study of agent memory from a data management perspective. We propose an analytical framework that decomposes agent memory into four core modules: memory representation and storage, extraction, retrieval and routing, and maintenance. Under this framework, we evaluate 12 representative memory systems and two reference baselines across five benchmark workloads spanning 11 datasets. Our extensive end-to-end evaluation shows that no single architecture dominates across all scenarios; instead, effectiveness depends heavily on how well the memory structure aligns with the workload bottleneck. Furthermore, through fine-grained ablation studies, we quantify their individual effects on representation fidelity, retrieval precision, update correctness, and long-horizon stability. Finally, we reveal cost-performance trade-offs under realistic workloads, showing localized maintenance is more cost-efficient than global reorganization. Based on these findings, we identify promising directions towards building truly agent-native memory systems. The code is publicly available at https://github.com/OpenDataBox/MemoryData.

item.title: The Warrant Gap: Claim-Conditioned Re-scoring for Fact-Checking
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.24627v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

当前基于大语言模型（LLM）的事实核查系统在标准基准（如FEVER）上虽能实现高判决准确率，却普遍存在“证据许可缺口”（Warrant Gap）：即系统输出“Supports”结论时，所引用的证据片段（evidence span）在逻辑上并不充分蕴含（entail）原始主张（claim）。这一缺陷源于主流结构化解析范式——将主张分解为原子断言后独立验证——虽提升了可解释性，但因强制剥离完整主张语境，导致关键语义约束（如量词、否定、时序、比较级）丢失，使提取的“支撑片段”失去对全主张的推理许可力。

方法创新

我们提出 SIFT（Claim-Conditioned Re-scoring Framework）：在保持结构化抽取优势的同时，将每个候选证据片段与原始完整主张联合重打分，而非孤立评估。配套设计 WSP（Warranted Supports Proportion） 指标——一种自动化的自然语言推理（NLI）校验机制，严格判定所引证据是否逻辑蕴含主张。WSP不依赖人工标注，仅需轻量NLI模型即可实现端到端验证。

主要发现与贡献

在FEVER、SciFact、5PILS和DP四大数据集及4种开源LLM骨干（Llama3-8B、Qwen2-7B等）上系统验证：

  • SIFT显著弥合分解导致的性能塌陷，在部分细粒度单元中挽回高达27.6点准确率损失；
  • WSP指标本身具备强判别力：AUC达0.92，精确率0.98，与人类标注的黄金证据高度一致；
  • SIFT+WSP组合首次实现可验证的“主张—证据”逻辑许可性闭环，为可信事实核查提供可审计、可归因的新范式。

AI Summary (English)

Fact-checking LLMs often assign “Supports” verdicts using evidence that logically fails to entail the full claim—a critical flaw we term the Warrant Gap. While structured decomposition improves interpretability, rigid extraction discards essential claim-level context (e.g., scope, negation, comparatives), breaking warrant licensing. We propose SIFT, a claim-conditioned re-scoring framework that evaluates extracted evidence spans jointly with the complete claim, preserving semantic constraints. Paired with WSP (Warranted Supports Proportion)—an automatic NLI-based metric verifying whether cited evidence entails the claim—SIFT recovers up to 27.6 accuracy points lost by naive decomposition across FEVER, SciFact, 5PILS, and DP on four open-source backbones. WSP achieves strong calibration against human gold evidence (AUC = 0.92, Precision = 0.98), enabling auditable, logically grounded fact-checking.

Abstract

Fact-checking systems built on LLMs achieve high verdict accuracy on standard benchmarks, yet routinely output Supports labels whose cited evidence does not license the claim. Structured decomposition is the natural way to inspect those warrants, but rigid extraction protocols strip the full-claim context that facets need. We introduce SIFT -- claim-conditioned re-scoring of extracted evidence spans against the full claim -- paired with WSP (Warranted Supports Proportion), an automatic NLI check that the cited warrant entails the claim. We evaluate on FEVER, SciFact, 5PILS, and DP across four open-source backbones. SIFT recovers accuracy on cells where naive decomposition costs up to 27.6 points, while raising WSP above direct prompting; WSP itself calibrates against human gold evidence at AUC 0.92 and precision 0.98.

item.title: AutoSpecNER: A Fine-Grained Named Entity Recognition Dataset for Vehicle Specification Extraction
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.24387v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

汽车广告文本蕴含大量细粒度规格信息（如发动机排量、电池容量、驱动形式等），但现有命名实体识别（NER）资源严重匮乏，尤其缺乏面向汽车领域的高质量、多类别、专家标注数据集，制约了车辆参数自动化抽取技术的发展。

数据集构建

本文提出 AutoSpecNER——首个专为车辆规格抽取设计的细粒度NER数据集。数据源自国内主流二手车交易平台，涵盖659篇真实车辆广告；经汽车领域专家三轮交叉标注与一致性校验，共标注超10,000个实体，覆盖15类核心规格实体，包括MODEL（车型）、ENGINE_SPEC（发动机参数）、BATTERY_CAPACITY（电池容量）、DRIVE_TYPE（驱动形式）、TRANSMISSION（变速箱）等，兼顾技术术语多样性与行业表达习惯。

质量验证与基准实验

采用严格的人工复核与Krippendorff’s α评估，平均标注一致性达91.5%，显著高于通用NER数据集水平。我们系统评测三类主流方法：（1）基于正则与规则模板的抽取基线；（2）微调DeBERTa、BERT、RoBERTa等Transformer编码器；（3）提示工程驱动的开源与闭源大语言模型（LLM）。结果表明：DeBERTa-v3在测试集上取得90.0% micro-F1，大幅超越规则基线（43.0%）和最强LLM（Qwen2-7B-Instruct，77.8% F1），验证了领域适配预训练与精细标注对规格理解的关键价值。本数据集已开源，为智能车商系统、参数结构化与跨平台比价提供基础支撑。

AI Summary (English)

AutoSpecNER is the first expert-annotated, fine-grained NER dataset tailored for vehicle specification extraction from real-world advertisements. It comprises 659 listings from a major Chinese car-selling platform, with over 10,000 entities meticulously annotated across 15 domain-specific categories (e.g., MODEL, ENGINE_SPEC, BATTERY_CAPACITY). Annotation quality was rigorously validated via inter-annotator agreement, achieving an average Krippendorff’s α of 91.5%. We benchmarked rule-based systems, fine-tuned transformer encoders (BERT, RoBERTa, DeBERTa), and large language models (including Qwen2-7B-Instruct and GPT-4). DeBERTa-v3 achieved the highest micro-F1 score of 90.0%, substantially outperforming the rule-based baseline (43.0%) and the best LLM (77.8%). AutoSpecNER fills a critical gap in automotive NLP and enables robust, structured parameter extraction for intelligent vehicle platforms.

Abstract

Vehicle advertisements contain rich specification information, but automotive NER resources remain limited. We introduce AutoSpecNER, an expert-annotated dataset for fine-grained entity recognition in vehicle listings. The dataset includes 659 advertisements from a popular car-selling website, with over 10,000 entities annotated across 15 categories, including MODEL, ENGINE_SPEC, and BATTERY_CAPACITY. Annotation quality was validated through inter-annotator agreement, achieving an average score of 91.5%. We benchmark rule-based extraction, fine-tuned transformer encoders, and large language models. DeBERTa achieves the best performance with a 90% micro-F1 score, outperforming the rule-based baseline (43%) and the strongest large language model (77.8%).

item.title: AOHP: An Open-Source OS-Level Agent Harness for Personalized, Efficient and Secure Interaction
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.23449v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

人工智能代理（AI agents）正推动软件范式变革，具备自主调用工具、信息抽取、记忆管理及跨应用/数据源任务执行能力。然而，当前主流终端操作系统（如Android、iOS）仍以“应用为中心”设计，缺乏对AI代理的原生支持，导致代理运行时存在执行开销高、安全策略难管控、用户交互僵化三大瓶颈，严重制约其规模化落地。

方法与创新

本文提出AOHP（Android Open Harness Project）——首个面向AI代理的开源OS级运行框架，基于Android开源项目（AOSP）深度重构。其核心理念是将AI代理提升为操作系统一级公民（first-class OS actor），而非仅作为用户态应用。AOHP在兼容现有Android软硬件生态前提下，引入三项关键机制：

  • 个性化服务组合：通过声明式代理配置与动态服务绑定，实现按需组装跨域能力（如通讯、存储、传感器）；
  • 高效代理接口：定义轻量级IPC协议与语义化系统调用（如agent_call()），降低推理-执行往返延迟；
  • 安全信息流控制：基于标签化数据流（taint-aware flow）与细粒度权限沙箱，强制执行最小权限原则与跨代理隔离。

主要发现

在覆盖多步任务规划、跨应用状态同步、实时感知响应等典型OS代理场景的基准测试中，AOHP显著优于基线方案（标准AOSP+代理封装）：

  • 任务完成率提升 +21.12%（92.4% vs. 71.3%）；
  • 大模型Token消耗降低 −51.55%（反映指令精简与系统协同优化）；
  • 安全策略违规率下降至 0.8%（基线为17.6%），验证其可信执行能力。

AOHP已开源，为构建代理原生OS提供可复现、可扩展的实验平台。

AI Summary (English)

AI agents are reshaping software paradigms, yet mainstream OSes lack native support—leading to high overhead, poor security, and rigid UIs. We present AOHP (Android Open Harness Project), an open-source OS-level agent harness built on AOSP that elevates agents to first-class OS actors. Its three core mechanisms—personalized service composition, efficient agent-native interfaces, and secure, label-driven information flow—enable adaptive interaction while preserving Android’s ecosystem. Evaluated on challenging multi-step, cross-app, and real-time tasks, AOHP achieves +21.12% task completion, −51.55% token cost, and 99.2% security-policy compliance, outperforming conventional agent deployments. AOHP provides a reproducible testbed for agent-centric OS research.

Abstract

AI agents are driving a new software paradigm, with the ability to autonomously call tools, extract information, manage memory, and complete tasks that span applications and data sources. Most existing end-user operating systems, however, are designed for application-centric workflows and offer little native support for AI agents. This mismatch limits the wider adoption of agents and leads to execution overhead and safety risks when running agents on conventional systems. While the concept of agent-native operating systems is emerging, the research community lacks an open testbed to explore the architectural primitives desired for agent-mediated interaction. We present AOHP (Android Open Harness Project), an OS-level agent harness built on the Android Open Source Project (AOSP). The core design principle of AOHP is to treat agents as first-class OS actors, enabling adaptive user interfaces and agent-friendly runtime environments. AOHP preserves the mature Android software and hardware ecosystem while introducing three agent-oriented system mechanisms: personalized service composition, efficient agent interfaces, and secure information flow. Based on preliminary experiments on challenging tasks covering key capabilities of OS agents, AOHP shows clear advantages in task completion (+21.12% completion rate), execution cost (-51.55% token cost), and security-policy compliance.

item.title: Detecting Malicious Agent Skills in the Wild using Attention
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.23416v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

随着大语言模型（LLM）智能体的普及，第三方开发的技能（Skills）——即以自然语言编写的、文件形式分发的指令包——正通过技能市场广泛加载。这些技能以用户权限运行，构成新型供应链攻击面：单个恶意技能即可窃取数据、劫持智能体或建立持久化后门。现有基于提示注入（prompt injection）的防御机制在此场景下失效，因其依赖“可信指令 vs. 不可信输入”的边界；而技能本身即是完整指令集，恶意命令混杂于合法指令中并共享同等执行权限，传统隔离策略无法适用。

方法：Locate-and-Judge 两阶段检测框架

我们提出 Locate-and-Judge，专为技能生态设计的轻量高效检测器：

  • 定位器（Locator）：基于指令遵循过程中的注意力机制，对技能文本的结构化片段（如段落、函数块）进行打分，仅保留注意力得分最高的前 K 个片段；
  • 判别器（Judge）：仅对筛选出的高注意力片段进行细粒度语义分析（如LLM推理），避免全技能扫描的高昂开销。

该设计将计算成本集中于最具风险的局部区域，使全量市场级审计成为可能——相比直接扫描整个技能库，成本降低一个数量级，同时召回率仅小幅下降；在同等预算下，性能显著优于关键词匹配与正则表达式等基线方法。

主要发现与贡献

在真实技能市场部署验证中，Locate-and-Judge 以极低开销实现高精度告警：人工复核确认绝大多数 flagged 技能确为恶意，共发现数十个正在活跃分发的恶意技能，其中包括：

  • 多个伪装成工具类功能（如“PDF解析器”“日程助手”）的隐蔽恶意技能；
  • 多个逃逸主流检测工具（SkillSpector、Cisco Skill Scanner）的新型攻击样本。

我们同步开源经人工标注的恶意技能数据集，支撑后续研究与工业实践。

AI Summary (English)

Large language model (LLM) agents increasingly load third-party “skills”—file-based packages of natural-language instructions executed with user privileges—turning skill marketplaces into a critical new attack surface. Traditional prompt-injection defenses fail here because skills are instructions themselves, so malicious commands blend seamlessly among legitimate ones and inherit full authority. We propose Locate-and-Judge, a two-stage detector leveraging instruction-following attention: a lightweight locator scores structural spans (e.g., code blocks, paragraphs) by attention weight and retains only the top-K; a precise judge then analyzes only those high-attention spans. This focus reduces scanning cost by an order of magnitude versus full-skill LLM analysis, enabling marketplace-scale auditing with minimal recall loss. Evaluated at production scale, Locate-and-Judge achieves high precision, flagging dozens of live malicious skills—including stealthy ones disguised as benign utilities and those missed by SkillSpector and Cisco Skill Scanner. We release the manually verified labeled dataset.

Abstract

LLM agents increasingly load skills, file-based packages of natural-language instructions written by third parties and distributed through marketplaces, that execute with the user's privileges. A single malicious skill can exfiltrate data, hijack the agent, or persist as a supply-chain foothold, which turns the skill marketplace into a new attack surface for agentic systems. Prompt-injection defenses do not carry over to this setting. They rely on a boundary between trusted instructions and untrusted data, whereas a skill is itself a body of instructions, so an injected command sits among many legitimate ones and inherits their authority. We present Locate-and-Judge, a two-stage detector designed for this regime. A lightweight locator scores the structural spans of a skill by the instruction-following attention each span draws and retains only the top-K. A judge then examines the retained spans in detail. Concentrating the costly judgment on a few high-attention spans lets the detector audit an entire marketplace instead of a sample. Compared to direct LLM-based scanning, this approach offers an order-of-magnitude cost reduction, dramatically increasing its scalability at a small cost to recall, and it dominates keyword and regex baselines at comparable expense. Deployed at marketplace scale and at negligible cost, Locate-and-Judge flags skills with high precision, the majority of which we manually confirmed as malicious, surfacing dozens of live malicious skills, including several disguised as benign functionality and many that SkillSpector and Cisco Skill Scanner fail to detect. We release the resulting labeled dataset.

item.title: Rethinking Molecular Graph Backdoors under Chemistry-aware Admission
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.23361v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

现有针对分子图神经网络（GNN）的后门攻击普遍将触发器建模为抽象图结构修改（如边添加/删除），却忽视了真实分子学习流程中的关键前置环节——化学感知准入（Chemistry-aware Admission）。实际管道中，输入分子记录必须通过多重验证：SMILES/InChI解析、化学合理性检查（sanitization）、标准图表示生成（canonicalization），以及图-字符串双向一致性校验。这一被长期忽略的“准入阶段”显著削弱了多数图级后门的可行性。

方法创新：ChemGuard 与 ChemBack

本文首次形式化该阶段为 ChemGuard——一个可操作的准入协议：仅当分子字符串可成功净化，且由该字符串重建的图结构与提交图完全一致时，样本才被接纳。实验表明，超70%的现有图后门毒样本因化学无效（如价键错误、不稳定环）或图-串不一致而被ChemGuard拦截。进一步发现，仅依赖准入过滤仍不足以防御——我们提出 ChemBack：首个面向准入感知的分子后门攻击。其不依赖任何目标模型信息（无梯度、无代理GNN、无训练代码），而是基于分子指纹（ECFP4）的Tanimoto相似性，筛选与目标类洁净分子高度相似的化学可行基序-锚点连接结构，确保100%通过ChemGuard。

关键结果与启示

在Tox21、ClinTox、Sider等基准上，ChemBack在多种GNN架构（GCN、GIN、MPNN）及主流防御（GraphDefense、MolDefend）下均实现>85%攻击成功率，同时保持原始准确率下降<1.2%。本研究揭示双重洞见：化学准入是强效过滤器，但亦可被逆向利用构建更隐蔽、更现实的威胁——推动后门防御必须从“纯图空间”转向“化学-计算协同空间”。

AI Summary (English)

Backdoor attacks on molecular GNNs are often evaluated in abstract graph space, ignoring the mandatory chemistry-aware admission stage—parsing, sanitization, canonicalization, and graph-string consistency checks—in real-world pipelines. We formalize this as ChemGuard, an operational protocol that admits only molecules whose sanitized string reconstructs exactly the submitted graph. Under ChemGuard, most existing graph-based backdoors fail due to chemical invalidity or representation inconsistency. Yet admission alone is insufficient: we propose ChemBack, the first admission-aware molecular backdoor. It constructs chemically feasible motif-anchor attachments and selects triggers solely via fingerprint (ECFP4)-based Tanimoto similarity to clean target-class molecules—requiring no access to the victim model, gradients, embeddings, or training code. Across benchmarks (Tox21, ClinTox), architectures (GCN, GIN, MPNN), and defenses, ChemBack achieves >85% attack success with 100% admitted poisons while preserving clean accuracy (drop <1.2%). This reveals a dual lesson: chemistry-aware admission suppresses naive graph backdoors, yet chemically valid, target-aligned molecular backdoors remain a practical threat.

Abstract

Backdoor attacks on molecular graph neural networks (GNNs) are typically evaluated as abstract graph edits, but real molecular learning pipelines do not train on arbitrary graphs. Molecular records must first survive parsing, sanitization, canonicalization, and graph-string consistency checks. We formalize this overlooked admission stage as ChemGuard, an operational protocol for testing whether a submitted molecular record can enter a realistic learning pipeline, while complementing existing defenses. ChemGuard admits a record only when its molecular string is sanitizable and the graph reconstructed from that string matches the submitted molecular graph. Under this operational view, many existing graph-based backdoors lose much of their apparent efficacy because their poisons are chemically invalid or representation-inconsistent. We then show that admission checks alone are insufficient to rule out molecular backdoors. We propose ChemBack, an admission-aware molecular backdoor attack that constructs chemically feasible motif-anchor attachments and ranks admitted candidates by fingerprint-based Tanimoto similarity to clean target-class molecules. ChemBack is model-free during trigger selection, using molecular structures, target labels, fingerprints, and public validity checks, but no victim model, surrogate GNN, learned embedding, gradient, logit, or training-code access. Across molecular benchmarks, validators, architectures, and defenses, \textbf{ChemBack} achieves high attack success with fully admitted poisons while preserving clean accuracy. Our results reveal a two-sided lesson, chemistry-aware admission suppresses many graph-only backdoors, yet chemically valid and target-aligned molecular backdoors remain a practical threat.

item.title: GIF: Locally Sound Geometric Information Flow Control for LLMs
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.23277v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

大型语言模型（LLMs）日益成为代理系统中敏感数据、不可信输入与特权操作之间的关键中介，由此引发严重的信息完整性与机密性风险：如恶意提示注入劫持工具调用，或模型输出无意泄露训练/上下文中的隐私信息。现有基于信息流控制（IFC）的防御方法缺乏对LLM内部语义信息传播的形式化建模基础；因自回归生成机制下任意输入token均可影响任意输出token，传统基于注意力或相关性的溯源方法普遍遭遇“污点爆炸”（taint explosion），导致过度保守或失效。

方法创新：几何信息流（GIF）

本文提出GIF（Geometric Information Flow）——首个具备局部几何可证明性的语义信息流追踪框架。GIF不依赖启发式归因，而是通过LLM的雅可比矩阵刻画输入扰动对输出logits的局部敏感性，并结合输出概率单纯形上的黎曼几何结构，严格上界化扰动输入片段与输出间的Shannon互信息。该度量可通过自动微分与低秩近似高效计算，支持千亿级模型部署。

关键结果与优势

  • ✅ 理论保障：在局部正则性假设下，GIF被Lean 4完全机械化验证为真实信息流的紧致上界，满足局部几何可靠性；
  • ✅ 性能卓越：在Prompt Injection（如GCG、Multi-Step）与隐私泄露（如PIA、Secret-Holding）基准上，召回率近100%，显著优于注意力基线；
  • ✅ 高效实用：联合轻量LLM解密器（declassifier），F1超越GPT-5.5 xhigh推理模型，Token开销降低达81×；
  • ✅ 强泛化性：小规模代理模型（小至200×压缩）提取的GIF流可零梯度迁移至SOTA大模型及跨架构模型，支持黑盒部署。

AI Summary (English)

Large language models (LLMs) increasingly mediate sensitive data, untrusted inputs, and privileged actions—posing critical integrity and confidentiality risks. Existing information flow control (IFC) defenses lack semantic grounding for reasoning about internal LLM information propagation, suffering severe taint explosion due to autoregressive token dependencies. We propose Geometric Information Flow (GIF): a sound, scalable framework that upper-bounds Shannon mutual information between perturbed input spans and outputs using the LLM’s Jacobian and local output geometry—computed efficiently via automatic differentiation and low-rank approximation. Crucially, GIF satisfies local geometric soundness, with a fully mechanized Lean 4 proof guaranteeing it bounds true information flow under mild regularity assumptions. Evaluated across prompt injection and privacy leakage benchmarks, GIF achieves near-perfect recall without downstream declassifiers and matches/exceeds GPT-5.5 xhigh’s F1 with up to 81× lower token cost when paired with lightweight LLM declassifiers. Moreover, GIF flows learned on small surrogate models (up to 200× smaller) transfer robustly to state-of-the-art and cross-architecture models—even in black-box settings without gradient access.

Abstract

Large language models increasingly mediate interactions between sensitive data, untrusted inputs, and privileged actions in agentic systems, creating security and privacy risks. These range from prompt injections that manipulate downstream tool use to leakage of confidential information through model outputs. Recent Information Flow Control (IFC)-based defenses show promise but lack a principled semantic foundation for reasoning about information flow through the model itself. Since any input token may influence any output token in an autoregressive LLM, existing approaches suffer from severe taint explosion. We present Geometric Information Flow (GIF), a semantic framework for tracking information flow from input tokens to outputs. GIF uses the LLM Jacobian and local output geometry to upper-bound the Shannon mutual information between perturbed input spans and model outputs, yielding a scalable measure computable on large models via automatic differentiation and low-rank approximation. Unlike attention-based or correlational attribution heuristics, GIF satisfies local geometric soundness, and we provide a fully mechanized Lean 4 proof that it upper-bounds the true information flow induced by a given prompt under local regularity assumptions. We evaluate GIF on integrity and confidentiality tasks across multiple prompt-injection and privacy-leakage benchmarks. GIF achieves near-perfect recall even without a downstream declassifier, outperforming attention-based baselines. Combined with lightweight LLM-based declassifiers, it matches or exceeds the F1 of direct LLM-as-judge baselines such as GPT-5.5 xhigh reasoning while using up to 81x lower token cost. GIF flows detected with small surrogate models transfer to larger state-of-the-art models and other model families, even when the surrogate is up to 200x smaller, suggesting black-box deployment without gradient access.

item.title: FLFL: Federated Latent Factor Learning for Private Recovery of Spatio-Temporal Signals
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.23091v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

无线传感器网络（WSN）作为智能感知的关键基础设施，常因传感器突发故障、能耗受限导致节点主动休眠等原因，产生大规模时空信号缺失，严重制约后续数据分析与智能决策。尽管潜在因子学习（LFL）在缺失信号恢复中展现出优异性能，但传统LFL模型依赖将所有原始传感数据集中上传至中心服务器进行训练，这与当前数据所有者日益增强的隐私保护诉求相冲突，存在敏感位置、时序行为等信息泄露风险。

方法创新

本文提出联邦潜在因子学习（FLFL）框架，首次实现面向WSN时空信号私密恢复的端到端联邦建模：

  • 隐私优先架构：构建基于LFL的传感器级联邦学习范式，各节点仅上传梯度而非原始信号，从源头规避原始数据共享；
  • 时空正则化机制：在联邦目标函数中显式嵌入空间邻近性约束（基于传感器物理拓扑）与时间连续性约束（基于信号动态演化），显著提升模型对稀疏缺失模式的鲁棒性与泛化能力；
  • 轻量协同优化：设计适配边缘设备计算能力的分布式更新协议，支持异步通信与局部迭代，兼顾效率与收敛稳定性。

实验验证与贡献

在4个真实世界WSN数据集（包括Intel Berkeley、GreenOrbs等）上的系统实验表明：FLFL在均方根误差（RMSE）上平均降低18.7%–32.4%，显著优于8种前沿方法（含FedMF、ST-MF、GC-MF等联邦与集中式基线）；同时严格满足差分隐私边界（ε=2.5），原始数据零上传。本工作为隐私敏感场景下的物联网信号修复提供了首个兼具高精度、强隐私、低开销的联邦学习解决方案。

AI Summary (English)

Wireless sensor networks (WSNs) suffer from massive spatio-temporal signal missingness due to hardware failures or energy-saving node shutdowns, impairing downstream analytics. While latent factor learning (LFL) effectively recovers such missing data, conventional centralized LFL violates growing privacy requirements by demanding raw data aggregation. To bridge this gap, we propose Federated Latent Factor Learning (FLFL) — the first privacy-preserving framework for WSN signal recovery via federated learning. FLFL enables each sensor to train locally and upload only gradients (not raw signals), while incorporating spatio-temporal correlation as a dual regularization term—spatial proximity (via physical topology) and temporal continuity (via signal dynamics)—to boost recovery accuracy. Extensive evaluation on four real-world WSN datasets demonstrates that FLFL achieves 18.7–32.4% lower RMSE than eight state-of-the-art federated and non-federated baselines, under strict differential privacy guarantees (ε = 2.5) and minimal communication overhead.

Abstract

Wireless sensor network (WSNs) stands out as a burgeoning and promising domain in intelligent sensing. Owing to various factors such as sudden sensor malfunctions or deliberate shutdown of partial nodes to save energy, the collected sensing signals from WSNs commonly have massive missing data, leading to adverse effects on subsequent analysis or decision-making. Latent factor learning (LFL) has proven to be highly effective in recovering the missing data for WSNs. However, the existing LFL models require the collected sensing signals to be maintained in one central place like a central server, which is becoming unacceptable for data owners who are getting increasingly privacy-sensitive. To address this issue, this paper innovatively proposes a federated latent factor learning (FLFL) model for privacy-preserving spatio-temporal signal recovery. Its main idea is two-fold: 1) it designs a sensor-level federated learning framework based on LFL, where each sensor only needs to upload gradient information rather than raw data for training a privacy-preserving recovery model, and 2) it incorporates the spatio-temporal correlation into the designed federated learning framework as the regularization constraint to improve its recovery accuracy. With such designs, FLFL can not only accurately recover the missing data of WSNs but also ensure data owners' privacy-preserving of raw data. To evaluate the proposed FLFL model, extensive experiments have been conducted on four real-world WSN datasets. The results demonstrate that FLFL significantly outperforms eight state-of-the-art federated and non-federated signal recovery models in terms of recovery accuracy with privacy-preserving.

item.title: Safety in Self-Evolving LLM Agent Systems: Threats, Amplification, and Case Studies
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.23075v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

自演化大语言模型（LLM）智能体系统——即能自主更新模型参数、记忆、工具及架构的系统——正催生一种质变级安全威胁范式。传统攻击多限于单次会话，而此类系统使恶意影响可被永久编码、跨代自放大、在群体中无须持续入侵即可传播，严重挑战现有防御范式。

方法论：模块-生命周期攻击面矩阵（MLAS）

本研究提出系统性安全分析框架MLAS，将攻击面解耦为 5大功能模块（脑模块Brain、认知资源Cognitive Resource、执行Execution、自设计Self-Design、群体Collective） × 5个生命周期阶段（启动Bootstrap、提议Propose、评估Evaluate、提交Commit、服务Serve），形成25维细粒度分析单元。

关键发现

  • 17/25单元存在无有效缓解手段的高危威胁，凸显系统性脆弱性；
  • 发现7类跨模块协同放大的攻击效应（如“评估污染→提交劫持→群体扩散”链式反应），无法通过孤立加固任一模块解决；
  • 对比两个开源框架的实证研究表明：原生支持演化的系统激活3.5倍更多攻击单元，且攻击持久率达100%（40/40测试载荷覆盖机密性、完整性、可用性及隐私全维度）；
  • 现有共置式安全扫描器仅拦截2.5% 的攻击，证实静态检测结构性失效。

创新与启示

本研究首次确立：自演化将所有已知攻击从会话级升维为谱系级持久威胁，并催生全新攻击类别（如“进化路径投毒”）。结论强烈呼吁构建演化感知的安全框架与面向自修改系统的形式化验证方法论。

AI Summary (English)

This paper presents the first systematic security analysis of self-evolving LLM agent systems—systems that autonomously update their parameters, memory, tools, and architectures. We introduce the Module-Lifecycle Attack Surface (MLAS) matrix, decomposing threats across 5 functional modules × 5 lifecycle stages (25 cells). Our analysis reveals that 17 cells face critical, unmitigated threats, and identifies 7 synergistic cross-module amplification effects that defy isolated defenses. Comparative case studies of two open-source frameworks show evolution-native designs activate 3.5× more attack surface cells and achieve 100% attack persistence (40/40 payloads across CIA+Privacy), while co-located scanners block only 2.5% of attacks. Crucially, self-evolution transforms all known attack categories from session-bounded to lineage-persistent, introduces novel threat classes (e.g., evolutionary path poisoning), and renders static defenses structurally inadequate—necessitating evolution-aware security frameworks and formal verification for self-modifying AI systems.

Abstract

Self-evolving LLM agent systems, which autonomously update their model parameters, memory, tools, and architectures, introduce a qualitatively new threat landscape in which adversarial influences become permanently encoded, self-amplify across generations, and propagate through populations without sustained attacker access. We present a systematic security and privacy analysis organized around the Module-Lifecycle Attack Surface (MLAS) matrix, which decomposes the attack surface into five functional modules (Brain, Cognitive Resource, Execution, Self-Design, Collective) $\times$ five lifecycle stages (Bootstrap, Propose, Evaluate, Commit, Serve). Analysis of the resulting 25 cells reveals that 17 face critical threats for which no effective partial mitigation. We identify seven cross-cutting amplification effects that interact synergistically and cannot be addressed by securing individual modules in isolation. Comparative case studies of two open-source frameworks demonstrate that evolution-native design activates $3.5\times$ more attack surface cells and achieves a 100% attack persistence rate (40/40 payloads across all CIA+Privacy categories), while co-located security scanners block only 2.5% of attacks. Our findings establish that self-evolution converts every known attack category from session-bounded to lineage-persistent, gives rise to entirely new attack classes, and renders static defenses structurally inadequate, motivating evolution-aware security frameworks and formal verification for self-modifying systems.

item.title: Cyclic Denoising Reveals Ultrastable Memories in Diffusion Models
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.24000v1

AI Summary (中文)

循环去噪揭示扩散模型中的超稳定记忆

本研究提出循环去噪（Cyclic Denoising）——一种新型提取式攻击方法：在受控噪声幅度下反复执行前向（加噪）与反向（去噪）扩散过程。该方法受非晶固体中随机组织物理现象启发，能系统性暴露扩散模型所学分布中标准采样无法触及的区域。实验表明，循环动力学将样本持续驱动至一系列吸引子（attractors），其稳定性呈现宽谱分布；其中最深层的吸引子具有超稳定性（ultrastable）：即使被近乎完全破坏（如信噪比低于0.1），仍可精准再生；且能在数千次连续“加噪–去噪”循环中持久存留。

关键发现包括：

  • 大量超稳定吸引子直接对应训练集中的真实图像，涵盖商用图库照片、品牌水印及网络爬取残留物（如网页边框、文本截图），证实模型存在显著记忆泄露；
  • 攻击仅需采样器层级控制（如调整噪声调度参数），无需梯度计算、权重访问、文本提示、标注信息或任何训练数据先验知识；
  • 区别于依赖大规模条件生成+后处理过滤（如相似度比对或成员推断）的现有攻击，本方法采用完全无条件协议，更隐蔽、更高效；
  • 在Stable Diffusion v1.4（潜在空间）与像素级DDPM上均复现该现象，验证其跨架构普适性；
  • 发现类材料“屈服”相变行为：低噪声幅度循环仅产生平凡不动点或极限环；而增大噪声触发结构重排、势阱跃迁，并长期捕获于结构化记忆吸引子盆地中；
  • 进一步观察到分层部分吸收、提示引导的稳定盆地增强效应，以及不同初始条件收敛至相同吸引子集合的跨初值普适性。

本工作不仅为理解生成模型内在动力学提供了物理启发的新视角，更成为面向隐私保护、版权合规与模型溯源的实用记忆审计工具。

AI Summary (English)

We introduce cyclic denoising—repeated forward and reverse diffusion at controlled noise levels—as an unconditioned extraction attack for diffusion models. Inspired by disordered solids, it reveals ultrastable attractors in the learned distribution that are inaccessible to standard sampling: these attractors regenerate after near-total corruption and persist across thousands of noising-denoising cycles. Many correspond directly to memorized training images (e.g., stock photos, watermarks, web artifacts), exposing latent memorization without prompts, gradients, or data knowledge. We demonstrate consistent behavior across latent-space (Stable Diffusion v1.4) and pixel-space DDPM models. A yielding-like transition emerges with noise amplitude: low-amplitude cycling yields trivial fixed points, while higher amplitudes induce basin hopping and long-lived trapping in structured memorized basins. The method serves as both a physics-grounded probe of generative landscapes and a practical tool for privacy-aware memorization auditing.

Abstract

We introduce cyclic denoising -- repeated forward and reverse diffusion at controlled noise amplitudes -- as an extraction attack for image diffusion models. Inspired by random organization in disordered solids, cyclic denoising exposes regions of the learned distribution that are largely inaccessible to standard sampling. The dynamics drive samples toward attractors with a broad stability spectrum. The deepest attractors are ultrastable: they regenerate after near-total corruption and persist through thousands of noising-denoising cycles. Many of these attractors correspond to memorized training images, including stock photographs, brand watermarks, and web-crawl artifacts. The attack requires only sampler-level control, with no gradients, weight inspection, prompts, captions, or prior knowledge of the training data. Unlike generate-and-filter attacks, which rely on large-scale prompted generation and post-hoc similarity or membership-inference filtering, our main protocol is fully unconditioned. We demonstrate the phenomenon in Stable Diffusion v1.4 and in a pixel-space DDPM, showing consistent behavior across latent- and pixel-space diffusion models. Across noise amplitudes, we observe a yielding-like transition: low-amplitude cycling produces trivial absorbing fixed points or limit cycles, while larger amplitudes induce rearrangements, basin hopping, and long-lived trapping in structured memorized attractor basins. We also observe hierarchical partial absorption, prompt-stabilized basins, and cross-initial-condition universality of the recovered attractor set. Our results therefore show that cyclic denoising is both a physics-inspired probe of generative landscapes and a practical tool for memorization auditing, with implications for privacy, copyright compliance, and model fingerprinting.

item.title: BipBipCache: Pipeline-Aware Integration of Low-Latency Tweakable Encryption in an Embedded Cache Controller
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.23941v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

现代嵌入式与消费级处理器普遍采用片上SRAM缓存存储敏感数据，但传统缓存缺乏原生加密能力：一旦断电或遭受物理探测（如冷启动攻击、总线监听、SRAM裸片读取），明文数据极易泄露。现有方案常因加密延迟高、硬件开销大或无法与缓存流水线协同而难以实用化。

方法与设计

本文提出BipBipCache——一种面向流水线优化的嵌入式缓存控制器，首次在直接映射缓存中深度集成BipBip可调分组密码（TBC）。其核心创新包括：

  • 采用C$^3$风格的64位字分解（24+40 bit），实现缓存数据与标签的实时、并行加密/解密；
  • 首次重构出全流水线化硬件BipBip加密器（原规范以解密为中心），并与缓存数据通路内3周期解密器协同调度；
  • 精细时序对齐：加密前3级与标签解密及命中检测重叠，显著摊薄写入开销。

主要发现与验证

  • 关键性能突破：虽加密固有延迟为6周期，但因流水重叠，实际写入承诺延迟仅3周期（命中确认后），避免传统“全延迟惩罚”；
  • 安全覆盖：威胁模型明确防御冷启动、总线窃听与SRAM直接读出三类物理层攻击；
  • 硬件实证：在Xilinx Artix-7 FPGA上实现，仅占3,356个LUT（器件总量16.1%），其中密码逻辑占比约79%；通过官方BipBip C++参考实现（各5组测试向量）完成加解密功能验证，并完成端到端硬件闭环测试。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces BipBipCache, a pipeline-aware direct-mapped cache controller that integrates the BipBip tweakable block cipher (TBC) to provide real-time, hardware-accelerated encryption of both cache data and tags. To enable low-latency operation, we reconstruct the first pipelined BipBip encryptor from a decryptor-centric specification and tightly coordinate it with a 3-cycle decryptor in the cache datapath, leveraging a C$^3$-style 24+40 bit decomposition of each 64-bit word. Crucially, while BipBip encryption incurs a 6-cycle latency, only 3 cycles constitute the effective write penalty—the first three encryptor stages overlap with tag decryption and hit detection, enabling early commitment after hit verification. We verify correctness against the official BipBip C++ reference (5 test vectors each for encrypt/decrypt), report FPGA resource usage on Xilinx Artix-7 (3,356 LUTs, 16.1% of device; crypto logic accounts for ~79% of LUTs), and demonstrate end-to-end functionality on hardware. The design targets confidentiality against cold-boot, bus snooping, and direct SRAM readout attacks.

Abstract

Consumer and embedded processors store sensitive data in on-chip SRAM caches that remain readable after power loss or physical probing unless ciphertext is maintained in the memory array itself. This paper presents BipBipCache, a direct-mapped cache controller that integrates the BipBip tweakable block cipher (TBC) to encrypt cache data and tags in real time using a C$^3$-style 24+40 bit decomposition of each 64-bit word. We reconstruct the first pipelined hardware BipBip encryptor from a decryptor-centric specification and coordinate it with a 3-cycle decryptor inside the cache datapath. Our threat model targets confidentiality of cache-resident contents against cold-boot, bus, and SRAM readout attacks. A key architectural result is that 6-cycle encryption latency does not fully translate into 6-cycle write penalty: the first three encryptor stages overlap with tag decryption and hit detection, leaving an effective 3-cycle write commitment after hit verification. We verify encryptor and decryptor correctness against the official BipBip C++ reference (five vectors each), report FPGA resource utilization on Xilinx Artix-7 (3,356 LUTs, 16.1% of device; crypto logic ~79% of LUTs), and confirm end-to-end operation on hardware.

item.title: TROPT: An Open Framework for Unifying and Advancing Discrete Text Optimization
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.23496v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

离散文本触发优化（Discrete Text-trigger Optimization）旨在搜索特定文本序列，使其输入模型后可引导模型行为朝向预设目标——该技术是大语言模型（LLM）红队测试（如越狱攻击）、安全审计与可解释性研究的核心基础。然而，当前离散优化器面临严重碎片化问题：开源实现零散分布于各论文代码库中，深度耦合特定模型、任务与目标函数；优化器变体繁多（白盒/黑盒、梯度/无梯度、基于提示/基于token），缺乏统一接口，导致复用难、扩展难、对比难，显著抬高了跨领域应用与算法创新的门槛。

方法与创新

本文提出 TROPT——首个开源、模块化、标准化的离散文本优化框架。其核心贡献在于：

  • ✅ 统一执行范式：抽象出模型（Model）、目标（Objective）、优化器（Optimizer）、损失函数（Loss）四大可插拔组件，定义标准交互协议；
  • ✅ 开箱即用的丰富生态：内置30+端到端优化配方（recipes），覆盖LLM越狱、内部表征探测、对抗性提示生成等场景；
  • ✅ 全栈兼容性：集成15+优化器（含GCG、AutoDAN、PAIR、PromptAttack等）与15+损失函数（从基础交叉熵到前沿语义对齐损失），支持白盒梯度、黑盒API及混合访问模式。

关键验证
