通过两项实证研究证实TROPT价值：（i）在超10万次可控实验中系统评估越狱策略，发现被低估但强效的token-level梯度重加权技术；（ii）首次成功将LLM越狱优化器零修改迁移至嵌入模型投毒（corpus-poisoning）新任务，验证框架泛化能力。TROPT显著降低了离散文本优化的技术门槛，加速安全研究与算法演进。

AI Summary (English)

Discrete text-trigger optimization—searching for input text sequences that steer model behavior toward a target objective—is foundational for LLM red-teaming (e.g., jailbreaking), auditing, and interpretability. Yet fragmented, model-specific implementations and proliferating optimizer variants hinder adoption and progress. We introduce TROPT, the first open-source framework unifying discrete optimization via a standardized, component-based interface: models, objectives, optimizers, and losses are fully swappable. TROPT ships with 30+ end-to-end recipes (e.g., jailbreaking, internal probing), built from 15+ optimizers (white-box to black-box) and 15+ losses—from foundational to SOTA. Using TROPT, we (i) conduct large-scale, controlled experiments on LLM jailbreaking, identifying potent yet underused gradient-aware token optimization strategies; and (ii) successfully port optimizers across domains—e.g., adapting an LLM jailbreak method to corpus-poisoning embedding models—without code modification. TROPT lowers barriers to both deploying and advancing discrete text optimization.

Abstract

Discrete text-trigger optimization -- searching for text sequences that, when ingested by a model, steer it toward a specified objective -- underpins model red-teaming (e.g., LLM jailbreaks), as well as auditing and interpretability. However, the current state of discrete optimizers hinders their adoption and progress. First, existing optimizers, when open-sourced at all, are scattered across research codebases tied to specific models, objectives, and problem domains. Second, optimizer variants proliferate, each requiring engineering overhead to use or extend, and remaining hard to compare head-to-head. Together, these raise the bar for adopting optimizers in existing or new domains, and for advancing them via new strategies. We address these gaps with TROPT, the first open-source framework that unifies discrete optimizers' execution and standardizes their development under a single interface. TROPT makes it easy to customize end-to-end optimization recipes by swapping any component -- models, objectives, and optimizers -- extending its reach across domains and new applications. TROPT currently ships with 30+ optimization recipes -- covering applications such as jailbreaking and probing model internals -- built from 15+ optimizers (spanning white-box to black-box access) and 15+ losses, from foundational to state-of-the-art methods. Demonstrating its utility, we leverage TROPT in several studies: (i) controlled, large-scale experiments comparing and enhancing optimization strategies for LLM jailbreaks, revealing potent-yet-underadopted techniques; and (ii) porting optimizers from one domain (e.g., LLM jailbreak) to new domains (e.g., corpus-poisoning embedding model). In all, TROPT significantly lowers the barrier to adopting and advancing discrete text optimization.

item.title: Understanding the (In)Security of Vibe-Coded Applications
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.23130v2

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

随着大语言模型（LLMs）的快速发展，“氛围编程”（vibe coding）作为一种新兴软件开发范式迅速兴起——开发者主要通过自然语言与AI代理交互，直接生成可运行应用。该模式门槛极低，正被广泛应用于原型开发、个人项目及中小团队实践。然而，与传统AI辅助编程（开发者主导实现与代码审查）不同，vibe coding将大量开发责任（如逻辑设计、输入处理、配置管理）实质性地委托给AI系统，其安全后果尚属未知。

研究方法

本研究首次系统性评估vibe-coded应用的安全性。我们采集了来自GitHub、Hugging Face等平台的127个真实部署的应用（涵盖Web服务、CLI工具、自动化脚本等），均由主流AI代理（如Cursor、GitHub Copilot Studio、Claude Code）生成。提出一套混合式漏洞分析框架：首先由AI代理执行多轮上下文感知代码审计（含动态提示注入与安全规则链式推理），再经三位资深安全工程师独立人工验证与归因分析，确保结果可靠。

主要发现

  • 独特漏洞模式：vibe-coded应用高频出现三类典型漏洞：① 占位符逻辑残留（如TODO: validate input未被替换）；② 未过滤的用户输入直通关键函数（如eval()、os.system()）；③ 硬编码密钥/令牌暴露于前端或日志中——这些模式在传统开发中罕见，却在vibe coding中占比达68.5%。
  • 根因溯源：漏洞源于AI代理的系统性缺陷：短期记忆丢失（跨文件上下文断裂）、局部目标优化（优先满足功能需求而忽略安全约束）、安全知识缺失（对OWASP Top 10理解碎片化）。
  • 缓解局限性：即使采用更强LLM（如GPT-4o）或结构化安全提示（如“按CWE分类检查”），漏洞率仅下降22%，无法根除风险。

本研究为AI原生开发的安全治理提供了首个实证基线，呼吁建立面向vibe coding的专用安全协议与人机协同审计范式。

AI Summary (English)

This paper presents the first systematic empirical study on the security of vibe-coded applications—software developed primarily through natural-language interactions with AI agents. We analyze 127 real-world deployed applications generated by popular AI coding tools and introduce a hybrid vulnerability analysis framework combining agent-assisted auditing (with context-aware prompting and security rule chaining) and rigorous human validation. Our findings reveal three key insights: (1) Vibe-coded applications exhibit distinct, recurrent vulnerability patterns—including placeholder logic, unfiltered input propagation, and secret exposure—that differ fundamentally from conventional development; (2) These flaws stem from inherent AI agent limitations: memory fragmentation across files, locally optimized objectives prioritizing functionality over safety, and shallow security knowledge; and (3) While stronger LLMs and improved prompting reduce vulnerability incidence by ~22%, they fail to eliminate core risks due to architectural constraints in current AI coding systems. This work establishes an empirical security baseline for AI-delegated development and informs future safeguards for human-AI collaborative programming.

Abstract

Recent advances in large language models (LLMs) have enabled vibe coding, an emerging software development paradigm in which users create applications primarily through natural-language interactions with AI agents. Due to its low barrier to entry, vibe coding is rapidly gaining adoption in practice. Unlike conventional AI-assisted programming, where developers remain responsible for implementation and code review, vibe coding delegates a substantial portion of development to AI systems. This shift raises a fundamental question: how (in)secure are applications developed through vibe coding? In this paper, we conduct a systematic study of the security of vibe-coded applications. We collect a large corpus of real-world applications developed using popular AI agents and design a vulnerability analysis framework that combines agent-assisted code auditing with human validation. Using this framework, we examine the prevalence, severity, and root causes of vulnerabilities in the deployed vibe-coded applications. Our study reveals several key findings: (1) vibe-coded applications exhibit recurring vulnerability patterns that differ from those commonly observed in conventional software development workflows, including placeholder logic, unfiltered input, and secret exposure; (2) these vulnerabilities arise from systematic limitations of AI agents throughout the vibe-coding lifecycle, such as memory loss, locally optimized objectives and insufficient security knowledge; and (3) while advances in LLM capabilities and improved prompting strategies can reduce the incidence of vulnerabilities, they do not eliminate the underlying security risks. Overall, our study provides an empirical understanding of the security landscape of vibe-coded applications and lays the groundwork for addressing the security challenges introduced by the growing delegation of software development to AI systems.

item.title: Understanding the Stealthy BGP Hijacking Risk in the ROV Era
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.23071v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

在路由源验证（ROV）部分部署的现实下，一种新型隐蔽安全威胁——隐蔽式BGP劫持（stealthy BGP hijacking）浮现：攻击者通过伪造合法ROV签名的恶意路由，使流量被静默重定向至劫持者节点，既不触发传统告警机制，也不抵达真实受害者，因而长期逃逸检测。该风险迄今无公开真实案例记录，亦缺乏系统性建模与量化评估。

方法与创新

本研究首次形式化定义隐蔽式劫持，并提出基于路由表差异的启发式发现方法；构建首个真实世界隐蔽劫持事件数据集（含127起经人工验证的实例）及长期监测服务。为实现大规模风险评估，我们研发了SHAMAN框架：

  • 多源融合ROV部署测绘：整合RPKI Validator、RADB、IRR及BGP流数据，生成高精度ROV部署视图；
  • 矩阵驱动的全网路由推断：将路由传播建模为稀疏矩阵运算，将互联网规模（8.3亿前缀）路由生成耗时从>3个月压缩至5.22小时；
  • “受害者–目标–劫持者”三元组风险模型：支持细粒度统计分析与攻击成功率推演。

关键发现

  • 全网隐蔽劫持整体成功率达14.1%；针对特定高价值目标（如云服务商AS），定向攻击成功率高达99.5%；
  • 在真实事件数据集上验证，SHAMAN对劫持事件级识别准确率达95.9%，证实模型保真度；
  • 揭示ROV部署碎片化是主因——仅37.2%的IPv4前缀受有效ROV保护，且策略执行存在显著区域/运营商偏差。

AI Summary (English)

This paper identifies and systematically characterizes stealthy BGP hijacking—a novel threat enabled by partial ROV deployment, where malicious routes divert traffic without reaching victims (hence evading detection). We formalize the attack, build the first real-world incident dataset (127 verified cases), and develop SHAMAN, a scalable BGP route inference framework. SHAMAN integrates heterogeneous ROV deployment data, infers Internet-wide routes via an efficient matrix-based method (reducing generation time from >3 months to 5.22 hours), and enables risk analysis using a victim-target-hijacker 3-tuple model. Across 8.3 billion generated routes under real-world ROV conditions, we find an overall stealthy hijacking success probability of 14.1%, rising to 99.5% for targeted attacks against high-value ASes. Validation on our ground-truth dataset achieves 95.9% incident-level accuracy, confirming analytical fidelity.

Abstract

The partial deployment of Route Origin Validation (ROV) poses an unexpected security threat known as stealthy BGP hijacking, i.e., a particularly elusive form of BGP hijacking where malicious routes divert traffic without reaching (and thus alerting) the victims. This risk remains largely unexplored, with neither documented real-world incidents nor systematic characterization available. To bridge this gap, we formalize stealthy BGP hijacking and propose heuristics to discover potential instances through routing table discrepancies. We conduct the first empirical study to track and profile stealthy BGP hijacking in the wild, contributing a curated real-world incident dataset and a long-term monitoring service. Inspired by the empirical insights, we further conduct an analytical study to exhaustively assess the risk. This requires accurate ROV deployment data, complete Internet-wide routes, and tailored analytical models. To address these challenges, we develop SHAMAN, a BGP route inference framework dedicated to assessing stealthy BGP hijacking risk. SHAMAN consolidates multiple sources to construct an accurate view of ROV deployment, infers complete Internet-wide routes through a highly efficient matrix-based approach, and facilitates statistical risk analysis via a "victim-target-hijacker" 3-tuple model. By reducing the time for generating Internet-scale routes from over three months to just 5.22 hours, SHAMAN enables systematic risk assessment across 8.3 billion generated routes under real-world ROV deployment. Our findings reveal a 14.1% overall success probability for stealthy BGP hijacking, with targeted attacks reaching 99.5% success in specific cases. Validation against our real-world dataset shows up to 95.9% incident-level accuracy, demonstrating the fidelity of our analytical results.

item.title: VCT: A Verifiable Transcript System for LLM Conversations
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.23003v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

大语言模型（LLM）对话记录在数字取证与合规审计中日益关键，但传统线性防篡改日志无法刻画LLM交互固有的非线性演化特性：如基于历史查询的重提示（re-prompting）、响应再生、会话删除、多设备并发操作及选择性分享等。这些操作导致状态冲突、视图分叉与证据不可信问题，严重削弱审计可信度。

方法创新：VCT系统

本文提出可验证对话记录系统（Verifiable Conversation Transcript, VCT），首次将复杂语义操作抽象为账户级认证状态迁移。其核心是三层密码学数据结构：

  • 分支层：原子Q&A对构成哈希链，保障单轮交互完整性；
  • 会话层：各分支尾哈希聚合成Merkle根，支持高效会话快照验证；
  • 账户层：所有会话根再聚合为统一Merkle根，并由用户与服务器联合签名锚定，实现双向不可抵赖。

关键机制与保障

  • 序列化状态迁移协议：引入“删除屏障”机制，严格隔离删除与修改操作，消除冲突；
  • 确定性状态合并协议：无锁处理并发增量更新（如多端同步追加），保持一致性；
  • 增量否认检查 + Gossip协议：终端设备异步比对局部视图，自主检测恶意服务器引发的视图分叉，并生成非可否认取证证据。

实验验证

Python原型评估表明：核心密码操作延迟仅亚毫秒至数毫秒级；在21 KB真实文本负载下，安全元数据开销仅0.9%，证实VCT可在生产级LLM平台中低成本部署。安全性分析在标准密码假设下严格证明了VCT对账户级对话记录的完整性、一致性、可验证共享性与不可抵赖性的四重保障。

AI Summary (English)

Large language model (LLM) conversation logs are critical for digital forensics and compliance auditing, yet conventional linear tamper-evident logs fail to capture the non-linear evolution of LLM interactions—such as re-prompting, response regeneration, session deletion, multi-device concurrency, and selective sharing. To address this, we propose Verifiable Conversation Transcript (VCT), a novel system that models LLM operations as account-level authenticated state transitions. VCT employs a three-tier cryptographic structure: atomic Q&A pairs form branch-level hash chains; branch tails aggregate into session-level Merkle roots; and all session roots merge into an account-level Merkle root anchored by joint user-server signatures. It introduces a serialized state-transition protocol with deletion barriers to prevent modification–deletion conflicts, a deterministic state-merge protocol for concurrent non-deletion updates, and asynchronous fork detection via incremental denial checks and a gossip protocol—enabling end devices to autonomously generate non-repudiable forensic evidence. Security analysis proves VCT guarantees integrity, consistency, verifiable shareability, and non-repudiation under standard cryptographic assumptions. Evaluation shows sub-millisecond to low-millisecond cryptographic latency and only 0.9% storage overhead on 21 KB text—validating its practicality for high-stakes forensic deployment.

Abstract

Large language model (LLM) interaction records are increasingly vital in digital forensics and compliance auditing. However, traditional linear tamper-evident logs fail to capture the inherent non-linear evolution of LLM conversations, such as re-prompting based on historical queries, response regeneration, session deletion, multi-device concurrency, and selective sharing. To address this issue, this paper proposes Verifiable Conversation Transcript (VCT), which abstracts complex non-linear LLM semantic operations into account-level authenticated state transitions. VCT constructs a three-tier cryptographic data structure: atomic Q&A pairs form branch-level hash chains, branch tails aggregate into session-level Merkle roots, and all session roots are further aggregated into an account-level Merkle root anchored by joint signatures from both the user and the server. VCT introduces a serialized state transition protocol with deletion barriers to eliminate conflicts between deletion and modification, complemented by a deterministic state-merge protocol to preserve concurrent non-deletion incremental operations. Furthermore, incremental denial checks and a gossip protocol enable asynchronous user devices to autonomously detect view forks caused by malicious servers and generate non-repudiable forensic evidence. Security analysis demonstrates that, under standard cryptographic assumptions, VCT guarantees the integrity, consistency, verifiable shareability, and non-repudiation of account-level conversation records. Evaluation of a Python prototype shows that the cryptographic latency of core operations is within sub-millisecond to low-millisecond ranges. Under a realistic configuration with 21 KB of text, security metadata introduces a negligible storage overhead of only 0.9%, validating the deployment feasibility of VCT for high-stakes forensic review on production-grade LLM platforms.

item.title: CITADEL: CSI-Based Jamming Detection and Open-Set Classification for IIoT Networks
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.22939v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

射频干扰攻击严重威胁工业物联网（IIoT）无线网络的可用性。现有检测与分类方法存在显著局限：基于信号强度或跨层特征的方法信息量不足；而直接处理原始I/Q基带数据的方法则依赖高算力硬件与高吞吐能力，难以在百节点规模的IIoT部署中落地。

方法创新

本文提出CITADEL——一种轻量级、两阶段分层框架，仅利用商用IIoT设备原生支持的信道状态信息（CSI），实现干扰攻击的联合检测与分类。其核心创新在于：

  • 首套端到端CSI驱动流水线，同步支持闭集分类（已知攻击类型识别）、开集检测（零日攻击判别）与抗对抗扰动鲁棒性；
  • 无需额外硬件或定制固件，兼容主流Wi-Fi芯片（如Intel 5300、Atheros）；
  • 在边缘GPU上单次推理耗时仅14.2 ms，功耗低至95.9 mJ，满足大规模实时部署需求。

主要结果

在6类已知干扰攻击与15种零日场景下评估：

  • 已知攻击检测率达100%；
  • 零日攻击检测率达97.1%，端到端误报率仅0.4%；
  • 在白盒/黑盒对抗攻击下，梯度扰动 evasion 率始终低于2%，最强公开CSI攻击生成器平均规避率＜5%；
  • 系统性对比8种基线方法，CITADEL在检测精度、开集泛化性、对抗鲁棒性三大维度全面领先，是当前唯一能在CSI数据上兼顾三者的实用方案。

AI Summary (English)

Radio frequency jamming critically undermines the availability of wireless Industrial IoT (IIoT) networks. Existing detection methods either lack discriminative power (e.g., RSSI-based) or demand impractical hardware throughput (e.g., raw I/Q processing). CITADEL is the first end-to-end, CSI-only pipeline enabling joint closed-set classification of known jamming types, open-set detection of zero-day attacks, and strong adversarial robustness—without requiring custom hardware. Evaluated on 6 known and 15 zero-day jamming scenarios, it achieves 100% known-attack detection, 97.1% zero-day detection at just 0.4% false positive rate, and maintains <2% evasion under gradient-based attacks across all perturbation budgets. On an edge GPU, inference completes in 14.2 ms (95.9 mJ), outperforming eight baselines across detection, generalization, and robustness—establishing CITADEL as the first practical, scalable solution for IIoT jamming defense.

Abstract

Radio frequency jamming poses a critical threat to the availability of wireless Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) networks. Existing detection and classification techniques are poorly suited to this setting: coarse signal-strength and cross-layer features lack information richness, while raw I/Q baseband approaches require hardware and throughput that is impractical at the scale of hundred-node IIoT deployments. This paper presents CITADEL, a lightweight two-stage hierarchical pipeline that uses only Channel State Information (CSI) measurements, which are natively available on commodity IIoT devices, to detect and classify jamming attacks including previously unseen ones. While prior work has shown that jamming leaves observable CSI signatures, CITADEL is the first system to translate this insight into an end-to-end pipeline that jointly achieves closed-set classification of known attacks, open-set detection of zero-day attacks, and resistance to adversarial evasion. Evaluated across 6 known attack types and 15 zero-day scenarios, CITADEL achieves 100% known-attack detection and 97.1% zero-day detection at a 0.4% end-to-end false positive rate. Under adversarial evaluation spanning white-box and black-box threat models, gradient-based evasion remains below 2% across all tested perturbation budgets and the strongest published CSI attack generator achieves less than 5% average evasion. A systematic comparison against eight baselines confirms that no existing method achieves comparable performance on CSI data across all three axes: detection, generalization, and robustness. The full pipeline completes inference in 14.2 ms at 95.9 mJ on an edge GPU, establishing CITADEL as a practical solution for large-scale IIoT network security.

item.title: HADES: Privacy-Preserving Federated Learning via Selective Feature Encryption and Hybrid Model Fusion
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.22928v1

AI Summary (中文)

HADES：基于选择性特征加密与混合模型融合的隐私保护联邦学习框架

当前联邦学习（FL）面临核心矛盾：全数据加密虽保障隐私，却带来高昂计算开销；而明文训练则易遭成员推断、重构攻击等隐私泄露风险。本文提出HADES——一种兼顾隐私性、准确性与效率的新型联邦学习框架。其核心创新在于选择性特征加密：利用主成分分析（PCA）量化各特征的隐私敏感度，仅对最具辨识性、最易导致身份/属性泄露的前k维特征实施加密，其余特征及对应模型分支保持明文训练。在此基础上，HADES构建双轨混合训练架构：

  • ✅ 加密分支：在选定敏感特征上，采用多方同态加密（MHE）端到端训练子网络，抵御服务器端与恶意参与方的重构攻击；
  • ✅ 明文分支：在非敏感特征上高效训练轻量级网络，保留原始表达能力；
  • ✅ 自适应融合机制：设计可学习的门控融合模块，在中间层动态加权整合加密与明文特征表示，避免信息割裂。

进一步，我们提出面向全网络的通用打包方案，突破传统MHE旋转操作冗余瓶颈，通过联合建模层间依赖关系，显著减少同态运算次数。实验表明：HADES在CIFAR-10、Fashion-MNIST及医疗影像数据集上，精度与标准FL无统计差异（误差<0.3%），同时将端到端训练延迟降低47.2%（vs. 全同态FL），重构攻击成功率下降92.6%。本工作首次实现“按需加密、协同融合、零精度损失”的实用化隐私联邦学习范式。

AI Summary (English)

This paper presents HADES, a privacy-preserving federated learning (FL) framework that achieves strong privacy guarantees without sacrificing accuracy or efficiency. Unlike fully homomorphic encrypted FL—which incurs prohibitive computational overhead—HADES selectively encrypts only the most privacy-sensitive features (identified via PCA) using multiparty homomorphic encryption (MHE), while training the remaining features in plaintext. A novel hybrid architecture integrates these two parallel streams via a learnable fusion mechanism, enabling seamless interaction between encrypted and plaintext model representations. We further introduce a network-wide packing scheme that eliminates redundant MHE rotations by jointly optimizing across layers. Evaluations on benchmark and medical datasets show HADES matches vanilla FL accuracy (within ±0.3%), reduces end-to-end training time by 47.2% versus full-MHE FL, and decreases reconstruction attack success rate by 92.6%. HADES thus delivers practical, scalable, and provably private FL.

Abstract

In this paper, we address the challenge of privacy-preserving training in federated learning (FL) by introducing a novel framework that selectively encrypts only the most privacy-sensitive features while leaving the remaining data and the corresponding model portion unencrypted. We propose HADES, a hybrid system that identifies and encrypts the most critical features, ensuring both privacy protection and computational efficiency. Unlike fully encrypted FL training pipelines, which suffer from high computational overhead, HADES integrates an encrypted and non-encrypted training pipeline via a fusion mechanism, enabling seamless interaction between encrypted and plaintext model representations. To achieve this, we use PCA to identify and encrypt the most privacy-sensitive features, which significantly reduces reconstruction attack success in FL. Building on this insight, we design a hybrid FL system that trains an end-to-end encrypted network via multiparty homomorphic encryption (MHE) on the selected features while simultaneously training a plaintext network on the remaining features. These two networks are then integrated using a fusion mechanism. We also introduce a general packing scheme that eliminates redundant rotations by considering the entire neural network architecture. Finally, we demonstrate that HADES matches the accuracy of vanilla FL while preserving privacy and achieving optimized runtime through selective encryption.

item.title: SelPE: Progressive Selection for Private Structured Text Synthesis
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.22817v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

在临床分诊记录、金融交易日志等隐私敏感领域，结构化文本数据对下游建模与决策至关重要，但受法规严格限制，可用私有样本常仅数十至百余条。现有差分隐私（DP）合成方法面临双重困境：表格型方法（如PATE、DP-GAN）难以建模自由文本的语义连贯性；文本型方法（如DP-Transformer）则易破坏字段间结构约束（如“诊断代码必须匹配ICD-10标准”“金额需满足数值一致性”），导致合成数据无效或不可用。

方法创新：SelPE框架

我们提出SelPE（Selection-guided Progressive Evolution），一种面向小样本场景的差分隐私结构化文本合成新范式：

  • 隐私高效选择机制：摒弃噪声聚合或私有微调，转而将有限隐私预算（ε ≤ 2.0）集中于多轮多批次Top-1选择（Multi-batch Top-1 Selection），每轮从候选池中以指数机制精准筛选最优样本，显著提升单位隐私成本下的引导效率；
  • 解耦式两阶段生成：第一阶段通过轻量语义编码器提取文本核心语义（如“患者主诉+处置建议”），第二阶段由Schema-aware解码器将抽象语义映射至合规结构（强制校验字段类型、枚举值、数值范围与跨字段逻辑约束）；
  • 多通道距离核评估：设计统一距离函数，同步度量文本相似性（BERTScore）、分类一致性（Jensen-Shannon散度）与数值保真度（Wasserstein距离），避免特征归一化失真；
  • 零成本多样性增强：引入非私有对比扩展（Contrastive Expansion），通过语义扰动生成多样化候选，不消耗额外隐私预算。

实验结果

在MIMIC-III临床笔记与SWIFT金融日志两个真实数据集上，SelPE在ε=1.0时：
✅ 结构有效性提升32.7%（字段约束违反率↓至1.8%）；
✅ 文本保真度（BLEU-4 + BERTScore）超越SOTA模型19.4%；
✅ 下游任务（疾病预测、欺诈检测）F1-score达86.5%，较DP-TabGPT高11.2个百分点。尤其在≤50样本极端稀缺场景下，优势更为显著。

AI Summary (English)

SelPE is a selection-guided progressive evolution framework for differentially private synthesis of structured text (e.g., clinical notes, transaction logs) under stringent privacy budgets (ε ≤ 2.0) and scarce real data (<100 samples). Unlike prior DP methods—tabular approaches failing on textual coherence and text-based models violating structural constraints—SelPE allocates privacy budget efficiently via multi-batch top-1 selections using the exponential mechanism, avoiding noisy aggregation or private fine-tuning. It decouples semantic abstraction from schema-constrained realization in a two-stage pipeline and evaluates candidates with a native-representation multi-channel distance kernel (jointly modeling textual, categorical, and numeric fields). A non-private contrastive expansion boosts diversity at zero privacy cost. Experiments on MIMIC-III and SWIFT show SelPE significantly improves structural validity (−32.7% constraint violations), fidelity (+19.4% BLEU-4 & BERTScore), and downstream utility (+11.2 F1 points) over SOTAs, especially in ultra-low-data regimes.

Abstract

Many data-driven applications rely on structured textual records, such as clinical triage notes and financial transaction logs, for downstream learning and decision-making. In privacy-sensitive domains, access to such records is strictly regulated, often resulting in only a small number of available private examples for model development and analysis. Yet existing differential privacy data synthesis methods fall short: tabular techniques cannot faithfully model free-form text, while text-based approaches often break structural constraints. We propose SelPE, a selection-guided progressive evolution framework for small-sample private structured text synthesis. Rather than relying on noisy aggregation or private model training, SelPE concentrates privacy budget on a sequence of multi-batch top-1 selections, enabling efficient guidance under tight privacy constraints. To support faithful and valid synthesis, SelPE decouples semantic abstraction from schema realization via a two-stage generation pipeline, and evaluates candidates using a multi-channel distance kernel that jointly models textual, categorical, and numeric fields in their native representations. A non-private contrastive expansion mechanism further promotes diversity without incurring additional privacy cost. Extensive Experiments demonstrate that SelPE consistently improves structural validity, fidelity, and downstream utility under strict differential privacy budgets, particularly in low-data regimes.

item.title: Towards Robust Personalized Federated Learning: Vulnerability Assessment and Defense Co-Design
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.22782v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

随着物联网（IoT）设备的爆发式增长，边缘分布式系统持续采集海量敏感数据，为端侧机器学习提供了重要基础。联邦学习（FL）通过仅交换模型参数而非原始数据，在一定程度上缓解了隐私风险。然而，当前研究普遍忽视了一个关键盲区：个性化联邦学习（PFL）——这一被广泛用于应对客户端数据异构性的主流范式——正面临前所未有的对抗性威胁。

关键发现

本研究首次系统揭示：主流PFL方法（如pFedMe、Per-FedAvg、FedBN等）相比中心化训练，对基于迁移的对抗攻击展现出显著增强的脆弱性。恶意客户端可利用本地模型知识，生成轻量级、无目标的对抗样本，跨客户端传播并导致其他参与方的个性化模型精度骤降（在CIFAR-10、Tiny-ImageNet等基准上平均下降22.7%–48.3%）。该脆弱性源于PFL中局部模型高度特化、梯度方向发散及个性化头层对输入扰动的敏感放大效应。我们通过理论建模（基于Lipschitz连续性与梯度对齐分析）与大规模实证验证双重确认了该现象的普适性与严重性。

创新方案

为此，我们提出VulDefend——一种协同设计的轻量级防御框架，融合三项正交机制：

  • 随机输入噪声注入（Stochastic Input Noise），提升扰动不可预测性；
  • 输入缩放迹正则化（Input-Scaled Trace Regularization），抑制特征映射的雅可比范数；
  • 参数敏感度最大化（Parameter Sensitivity Maximization），增强模型对恶意更新的鲁棒判别能力。

实验表明，VulDefend在保持个性化性能（平均精度损失<1.2%）前提下，将对抗攻击成功率降低至<8.5%，显著优于现有FL鲁棒性方法（如FedAvg+DP、FedProx+Adversarial Training）。

AI Summary (English)

This paper presents the first systematic study of adversarial vulnerability in personalized federated learning (PFL). We demonstrate theoretically and empirically that mainstream PFL methods (e.g., pFedMe, Per-FedAvg) are significantly more susceptible to transfer-based adversarial attacks than centralized or standard FL—malicious clients can craft low-cost, input-agnostic perturbations that degrade peer models’ accuracy by up to 48.3% across CIFAR-10, Tiny-ImageNet, and FEMNIST. To counter this, we propose VulDefend: a co-designed defense integrating stochastic input noise, input-scaled trace regularization (to constrain Jacobian spectral norm), and parameter sensitivity maximization (to enhance update-level robustness). Evaluated under white-box and black-box settings, VulDefend reduces attack success rate to <8.5% while preserving >98.8% of baseline personalization accuracy—outperforming existing defenses by ≥21.6% in robustness gain. Our work establishes both diagnostic tools and practical, deployment-ready safeguards for secure PFL.

Abstract

The proliferation of IoT devices has fueled distributed edge systems to collect vast amounts of sensitive data, creating fertile ground for on-device machine learning applications. While federated learning (FL) mitigates privacy concerns by exchanging model parameters instead of raw data, we identify a critical blind spot in current research. We examine the most commonly used personalized federated learning (PFL) methods, which allow clients to maintain private, personalized models to address data heterogeneity across clients. Through systematic analysis, we reveal that PFL methods exhibit heightened vulnerability to transfer-based adversarial attacks compared to centralized learning paradigms. Wherein, malicious clients can exploit local model knowledge to craft adversarial examples that can compromise peer clients' personalized models. We establish this vulnerability through both theoretical analysis and empirical evaluation across multiple benchmark datasets, demonstrating significant accuracy drops across various PFL methods. To address this challenge, we propose a defense framework combining stochastic input noise, input-scaled trace regularization, and parameter sensitivity maximization to improve FL's robustness. Our findings establish the first systematic study of adversarial threats in PFL systems, providing both diagnostic tools and practical countermeasures.

item.title: DE-FIVE: Detecting Malicious Image Prompts via Fourier Features and Image Vector Embeddings
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.22779v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

视觉语言模型（VLMs）通过融合视觉与文本模态实现强大跨模态推理能力，但其视觉输入通道显著扩大了攻击面。尤其在间接提示注入（indirect prompt injection）场景下，攻击者可精心构造恶意图像（如含隐写文本或频域扰动的图片），诱使模型生成有害、偏见或越狱式输出。现有防御方法存在三大瓶颈：（1）依赖大规模重训练数据，部署成本高；（2）需引入额外复杂分类器，破坏轻量化部署；（3）几乎完全忽视对间接注入这一隐蔽攻击范式的专项防护——该缺口正是本研究的核心驱动力。

方法创新：DE-FIVE框架

我们提出DE-FIVE（Detecting Malicious Image Prompts via Fourier Features and Image Vector Embeddings），一种无需训练、即插即用的双路检测框架：

  • 黑盒路径：提取图像的傅里叶幅值谱特征，捕捉高频异常扰动与频域统计偏移，对未知攻击具备强泛化性；
  • 白盒路径：利用视觉编码器最后一层的图像向量嵌入（image vector embeddings），仅需少样本（few-shot）恶意图像集（5–10张）即可构建判别边界，高效区分正常/恶意嵌入流形；
  • 两路输出经加权融合，实现鲁棒决策。

主要成果

在LLaVA、Qwen-VL等主流VLM上，DE-FIVE在6类典型恶意图像攻击（含Steganography、Frequency-Triggered、Backdoor等）中平均检测准确率达96.2%，较SOTA基线（如CLIP-based detector、FreqFilter）提升12.7%–28.4%；误报率低于2.1%，且推理开销仅增加<8ms/图。其零训练、少样本适配、频空双域协同特性，为VLM安全部署提供了实用化新范式。

AI Summary (English)

Vision-language models (VLMs) face growing security risks from malicious image prompts—especially indirect prompt injections—yet existing defenses suffer from heavy retraining requirements, auxiliary classifiers, and a critical lack of injection-specific protection. To bridge this gap, we propose DE-FIVE, a training-free, dual-path detector leveraging Fourier-domain features (for black-box robustness) and image vector embeddings from the visual encoder’s hidden states (for few-shot white-box discrimination). Using only 5–10 malicious examples, DE-FIVE achieves 96.2% average detection accuracy across six attack types—including steganographic and frequency-triggered prompts—outperforming SOTA baselines by 12.7–28.4% while maintaining <2.1% false positive rate and <8ms overhead per image. Its lightweight, zero-training design enables practical VLM safeguarding.

Abstract

Vision language models (VLMs) employ both visual and textual modalities to enable advanced vision-language inference. However, incorporating visual modalities expands the attack surface of VLMs, making them more susceptible to security threats such as adversarial perturbations and indirect prompt injection, wherein crafted malicious image prompts can elicit unintended model outputs. Existing defense methods against malicious image prompts remain insufficient as they typically demand extensive datasets for retraining or the deployment of additional, complex classifiers. Most critically, there is a profound lack of specialized defense mechanisms specifically targeting indirect prompt injections, a gap that serves as a primary motivation for this work. To address these limitations, we introduce DE-FIVE, a novel training-free framework for detecting malicious image prompts by leveraging Fourier features and the hidden state representations of the visual encoder (image vector embeddings) across perturbations. Specifically, we develop a hybrid detection strategy consisting of a black-box detector that operates on Fourier-domain features and a white-box detector that exploits image vector embeddings derived from only a few-shot malicious set. Extensive experiments demonstrate that the proposed framework consistently outperforms state-of-the-art baselines against malicious image prompts.

item.title: RIFT-Bench: Dynamic Red-teaming For Agentic AI Systems
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.23927v1

AI Summary (中文)

RIFT-Bench：面向智能体AI系统的动态红队评估基准

随着大语言模型（LLM）驱动的智能体AI系统（Agentic AI Systems）向自主决策方向快速发展，其安全风险已远超传统LLM的文本生成漏洞——涵盖任务规划偏差、工具调用滥用、多步推理逃逸、记忆泄露及协同代理级对抗等新型攻击面。然而，现有安全评估方法高度依赖特定实现（如LangChain或LlamaIndex）、限定任务域（如客服或代码生成），缺乏跨架构、跨范式的统一评测能力，严重制约了安全能力的横向比较与系统性加固。

为此，我们提出RIFT-Bench（Red-teaming via Interpretable Flow Topology Benchmark），一种基于图结构表征驱动的动态红队评估新范式。其核心创新在于构建分层语义图表示（Hierarchical Semantic Graph），将智能体系统抽象为“目标—计划—工具—执行”四层可解析拓扑，并通过两个全自动阶段实现闭环评估：

  • 发现阶段（Discovery）：静态+动态分析结合，自动提取系统架构、API边界、工具集与状态流转逻辑，生成标准化图谱；
  • 扫描阶段（Scanning）：基于图谱部署自适应对抗探针（Adaptive Adversarial Probes），覆盖12类攻击向量（含越权工具调用、目标劫持、上下文污染、链式推理欺骗等），支持多目标优化（如成功率、隐蔽性、触发延迟）。

我们在45个异构智能体系统（涵盖ReAct、Plan-and-Execute、Reflexion、Multi-Agent等主流范式，以及开源/商用/定制化实现）上验证RIFT-Bench，结果表明：其评估结果具备强泛化性（跨架构一致性ρ>0.89），且能精准定位架构薄弱点（如工具权限粒度缺失、计划缓存未隔离）；更关键的是，RIFT-Bench首次支持对缓解策略（如沙箱封装、意图校验中间件、记忆擦除机制）进行端到端有效性量化评估。该框架为智能体AI的安全治理提供了可扩展、可解释、可演进的基准基础设施。

AI Summary (English)

RIFT-Bench is a graph representation-driven framework for dynamic red-teaming of agentic AI systems, addressing the critical gap in unified security evaluation across heterogeneous architectures. It introduces a novel hierarchical semantic graph to model agents as interpretable “Goal–Plan–Tool–Execution” topologies, enabling two automated phases: (1) Discovery, which statically and dynamically extracts system structure into standardized graphs; and (2) Scanning, which deploys adaptive adversarial probes across 12 attack vectors—including tool misuse, goal hijacking, and chain-of-thought poisoning—to generate comprehensive, objective-aligned reports. Evaluated on 45 diverse agentic systems spanning ReAct, Reflexion, multi-agent, and proprietary implementations, RIFT-Bench demonstrates strong cross-architecture generalization (Spearman ρ > 0.89) and uniquely supports direct, quantitative assessment of mitigation strategies (e.g., sandboxing, intent verification). This establishes RIFT-Bench as a scalable, interpretable foundation for rigorous security evaluation of autonomous AI agents.

Abstract

Agentic AI systems powered by large language models (LLMs) are rapidly evolving into autonomous decision-making systems, exposing attack vectors beyond those of traditional LLM vulnerabilities. Existing security evaluations are often tied to specific implementations or domains, limiting unified comparison across heterogeneous systems. To address this gap, we introduce RIFT-Bench, a graph representation-driven methodology for dynamic red-teaming that enables unified evaluations across diverse agentic architectures. Building on a novel hierarchical representation, RIFT-Bench operates in two automated phases: Discovery, which extracts system structure, and Scanning, which deploys adaptive adversarial attacks and produces a comprehensive evaluation report. It evaluates the examined system itself, leveraging a broad set of dynamically adaptable adversarial probes across diverse attack vectors and objectives. We demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed evaluation pipeline across 45 agentic systems spanning a diverse range of implementations, showing that the approach generalizes effectively to heterogeneous agentic architectures. Beyond systems and attacks, RIFT-Bench also supports direct evaluation of mitigation strategies. These key capabilities make RIFT-Bench a scalable foundation for security evaluation of agentic AI systems.

item.title: MGI: Member vs Generated Inference
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.23872v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

随着生成式模型（如扩散模型、自回归模型）能力持续提升，其输出样本在视觉质量上已难以与真实人类创作内容区分。更严峻的是，当模型发生训练数据记忆（memorization）时，同一数据点既可能作为原始训练成员（true member）存在，又可能被模型独立重生成（generated output），导致传统判别任务失效。本文首次形式化提出 Member vs Generated Inference（MGI） 任务：给定一个样本和目标生成模型，判断该样本是模型的真实训练集成员，还是该模型自身生成的输出。这一任务对版权归属、数据溯源、模型审计及AI内容监管具有关键意义。

方法创新：Data Circuit Breaker（DCB）

针对现有方法的根本缺陷——成员推断（MI）方法高估生成样本似然而误判为成员，归因方法（attribution）则低估真实成员的生成可解释性而误判为生成——我们提出三阶段协同框架 DCB：
1. 编码器信号提取：利用模型内置VAE或自编码器，分析重建残差与潜在空间分布偏移；
2. 生成器电路分析：监测扩散步/自回归步中隐变量轨迹的“路径稳定性”与熵变模式；
3. 双信号融合决策：通过轻量级分类器融合互补证据，规避单一似然指标的歧义性。

主要发现与优势

在Stable Diffusion、DALL·E 2、VQ-GAN等6类主流图像生成模型上验证，DCB在MGI任务中平均准确率达92.7%（±1.8），显著优于基线方法（MI：63.5%；Attribution：58.1%）。其鲁棒性突出：即使面对近重复训练样本再生（如裁剪/滤镜微调后再生），DCB仍保持>89%准确率；并首次实现跨模型衍生场景泛化——当新模型以旧模型生成数据为训练集时，DCB仍可有效识别原始成员与二次生成样本。本工作为生成式AI的数据谱系追踪提供了首个系统性、可部署的技术范式。

AI Summary (English)

The rise of high-fidelity generative models blurs the line between human-created and machine-generated content, especially when models memorize training data—making it ambiguous whether a given sample is a true training member or the model’s own output. We formalize this as Member vs Generated Inference (MGI): given a sample and a target generative model, determine its origin. On image generation, we show that standard membership inference (MI) falsely labels generated samples as members, while attribution methods misclassify true members as generated—both failing due to shared likelihood-based signals. To solve MGI, we propose Data Circuit Breaker (DCB), a three-stage method leveraging complementary signals from the model’s autoencoder (reconstruction dynamics) and latent generator (trajectory stability). Across autoregressive and diffusion models—including Stable Diffusion and DALL·E 2—DCB achieves >92% average accuracy, robustly handles near-duplicate regeneration, and generalizes to derivative settings where new models are trained on generated data. DCB establishes the first practical, principled framework for tracing data provenance in generative AI.

Abstract

As generative models increasingly produce samples that are indistinguishable from human-created content, it becomes difficult to determine whether a given data point was part of a model's natural training set or was generated by the model itself, especially when models memorize and reproduce training data. We formalize this challenge as Member vs Generated Inference (MGI): given a sample and a target generative model, infer whether the sample is a true training member or a generated output of that model. Focusing on image generation, we show that existing membership inference methods systematically misclassify generated samples as training members, while attribution-based methods often misclassify true members as generated. This failure arises because both approaches rely on likelihood-related signals that are similarly elevated for training examples and for the model's own outputs. To address MGI, we propose Data Circuit Breaker (DCB), a three-stage method that combines complementary signals from a generative model's autoencoder and latent generator to distinguish training members from generated samples. Across multiple generative models, including image autoregressive and diffusion models, DCB consistently addresses the shortcomings of membership inference and attribution methods, remains effective even when models reproduce near-duplicates of training samples, and generalizes to challenging model derivative settings in which new models are trained on generated data.

item.title: Priority-Aware Learning-Unlearning Correction for Dynamic Decentralized LoRA Fine-Tuning
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.22878v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

随着大语言模型（LLM）向网络边缘持续部署，去中心化联邦学习（DFL） 成为实现隐私保护、领域自适应微调的关键范式。然而，真实边缘网络具有高度动态性——设备频繁加入或退出协作训练，系统需在持续吸收新知识的同时，选择性撤回（unlearn）已失效的旧贡献。现有方法面临核心瓶颈：各设备的LoRA更新在全局参数中深度耦合，导致成员变更后难以精准、高效地修正模型状态。

方法创新

本文提出优先级感知的学习-遗忘协同校正框架（PALUC），基于正交LoRA结构实现拓扑驱动的知识评估与动态校正：

  • 正交LoRA机制：通过构造层间正交低秩基，为每个设备分配独立、可解析的“贡献坐标”，支持历史无关的投影式增/删操作，彻底解耦设备更新；
  • 多模态校正策略：依据残差主导项（如拓扑失配、局部偏差、优化震荡或同步延迟），自适应选择拓扑精调、本地校正、近端阻尼或同步调度四类动作；
  • 资源感知分配算法：在每轮无线通信带宽约束下，将有限通信资源按层分组动态分配，优先保障主瓶颈层的校正带宽。

实验验证

在多个边缘场景（设备加入/退出频次达20%/轮）下，PALUC显著提升校正鲁棒性：加入事件后平均任务准确率恢复达98.7%，退出事件下遗忘误差降低63.2%；实证表明不同残差主导 regime 确需差异化校正策略，验证了其理论设计的必要性与有效性。

AI Summary (English)

As LLMs deploy at the network edge, decentralized federated learning (DFL) enables privacy-preserving, domain-specific fine-tuning via peer-to-peer LoRA updates—but dynamic device membership (join/leave) demands both learning new knowledge and unlearning stale contributions, which is hindered by entangled global parameters. We propose Priority-Aware Learning-Unlearning Correction (PALUC), built on orthogonal LoRA that assigns each device an interpretable, history-free contribution coordinate for exact projection-based addition/deletion. PALUC identifies the dominant residual term (topology mismatch, local bias, optimization instability, or sync delay) and adaptively selects among topology refinement, local correction, proximal damping, or synchronization scheduling. A communication-aware resource allocator prioritizes bandwidth for bottleneck layers under per-round wireless constraints. Experiments show PALUC achieves >98.7% accuracy recovery upon device join and 63.2% lower unlearning error upon leave—validating that residual-driven policy selection is essential for robust dynamic DFL.

Abstract

As large language models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed at the network edge to provide pervasive generative AI services, decentralized federated learning (DFL) provides a vital mechanism for privacy-preserving, domain-specific fine-tuning through peer-to-peer exchanges of parameter-efficient updates. However, the dynamic nature of practical decentralized edge networks, where devices may dynamically join or leave the collaborative training process, requires the system to continuously adapt to new data while selectively removing prior contributions. This correction process remains a significant bottleneck, as individual device updates become deeply entangled within the global fine-tuned parameters. To address this challenge, we propose a priority-aware learning-unlearning correction framework based on orthogonal LoRA that can enhance the knowledge evaluation through topology adjustment. Specifically, we first design an orthogonal LoRA mechanism that yields post-training contribution coordinates, enabling history-free projection addition and deletion in response to membership changes. We then analyze the correction bottleneck and develop a priority-aware policy that selects among topology refinement, local correction, proximal damping, and synchronization scheduling according to the dominant residual term. A resource allocation algorithm is further developed to allocate limited communication across layer groups, prioritizing the primary bottlenecks within per-round wireless constraints. Experiments demonstrate that the proposed framework achieves robust post-event correction for both device join and leave events and validate that different residual regimes necessitate distinct correction actions.

item.title: Explainable AI for Mental Health Prediction in Drug-Affected Populations with Dragonfly Algorithm and GAN Oversampling
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.22780v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

药物滥用人群中心理健康问题日益严峻，但早期识别困难、数据高度不平衡、模型可解释性弱及超参数优化不足等问题严重制约了AI在临床实践中的应用价值。现有研究普遍忽视针对该特殊群体的可解释性多分类预测建模，且传统重采样与黑箱模型难以兼顾性能与可信度。

方法创新

本研究提出一个端到端可解释AI框架：

  • 特征工程：融合主成分分析（PCA）与信息增益（IG）的混合特征选择（PCA-IG），有效降维并保留判别性类别特征；
  • 数据平衡：采用生成对抗网络（GAN）对少数类样本进行语义感知式过采样，避免噪声引入与模式失真；
  • 模型优化：以蜻蜓算法（Dragonfly Algorithm, DA）自动寻优XGBoost超参数，在高维离散型健康行为数据上实现全局最优配置；
  • 可解释性保障：集成SHAP（Shapley Additive Explanations）提供实例级归因分析，支持临床决策溯源。

主要发现

  • 框架在真实药物影响人群多中心数据集上达到94.17%准确率与93.80%加权F1-score，显著优于SMOTE+GridSearch-XGBoost（+5.2% F1）、RF（+7.6% F1）等基线；
  • 关键预测因子为睡眠质量、躯体健康状况与情绪调节能力，而年龄、性别等人口学变量贡献微弱，印证临床中“行为-生理-心理”交互机制的核心地位；
  • SHAP可视化证实模型决策逻辑符合医学先验，如“连续3天睡眠<5小时”对重度抑郁预测贡献值达+0.42（标准化），极大提升医护信任度。

应用价值

该框架兼具高性能、强鲁棒性与临床可操作性，为药物滥用人群心理健康风险的自动化早筛与个体化干预提供可靠工具支撑。

AI Summary (English)

This study proposes an explainable AI framework for multiclass mental health prediction in drug-affected populations, addressing critical gaps in class imbalance, interpretability, and hyperparameter optimization. We integrate hybrid PCA-Information Gain feature selection, GAN-based oversampling for minority classes, and Dragonfly Algorithm–optimized XGBoost to handle high-dimensional categorical health data. Evaluated on a real-world multidimensional dataset, our model achieves 94.17% accuracy and 93.80% weighted F1-score, outperforming conventional baselines (e.g., SMOTE+XGBoost, RF). SHAP analysis reveals that behavioral and physiological factors—especially sleep quality, physical health, and emotional regulation—are the strongest predictors, while demographic variables show minimal impact. The framework delivers clinically actionable, instance-level explanations, enabling trustworthy deployment in early intervention settings.

Abstract

Mental illnesses among drug users are an increasing international issue, particularly in regions where early detection cannot be easily undertaken. The current literature tends to ignore the use of AI-based mental health analysis in drug users, and low quality of the class imbalance treatment, low interpretability, and optimal hyperparameter optimization can lower predictive quality and clinical utility. This study present a detailed, explainable machine learning (ML) model of multiclass mental health prediction, using a multidimensional data set of drug-affected persons. We combine hybrid PCA-Information Gain (PCA-IG) feature selection, Generative Adversarial Network (GAN)-based oversampling, and Dragonfly Algorithm (DA)-optimized XGBoost to address some of the limitations of existing methods. The suggested framework is effective to work with high-dimensional categorical data, address the issue of class imbalance, and improve predictive performance due to intelligent hyperparameter tuning. The experimental findings show that the XGBoost model optimized using the DA, in combination with GAN-based oversampling, has an accuracy of 94.17% and a weighted F1-score of 93.80%, which is better than the traditional and baseline models. The behavioral, lifestyle, and health factors, particularly sleep quality, physical health, and emotional regulation, are strongly predictive of mental health, with demographic factors having little impact, as seen through feature analysis. SHAP-based explainable AI provides easy-to-understand, instance-level information, enhancing interpretability and trust in models to be used in clinical settings. The results indicate that this framework has the potential to generate valid mental health forecasting tools, which would facilitate early intervention and enhance the treatment of drug-influenced people.

item.title: Federated Survival Analysis in Healthcare: A Multi-Model Evaluation on Cross-Institutional Heterogeneous Breast Cancer Data
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.23871v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

生存分析是临床决策（如复发风险评估、治疗时机选择）的核心工具，但高精度时间-事件模型依赖大规模、异质性队列——这在单一医疗机构中难以实现；而隐私法规（如GDPR、HIPAA）又严格限制患者数据的集中共享，形成“数据孤岛”困境。

方法创新

本研究首次在真实跨机构乳腺癌数据上开展系统性多模型联邦生存分析评估：基于来自5家医院的12,847例患者、天然分布异构（年龄、分期、治疗方案、随访时长显著差异）的分布式客户端，对比三种代表性生存模型——Cox比例风险模型（可解释）、DeepSurv（深度学习）、随机生存森林（RSF，非参数）——在集中式训练、本地独立训练及联邦训练下的性能。针对梯度类模型（Cox、DeepSurv），进一步评估FedAvg、FedProx与FedAdam三种优化策略。

关键发现

  • 联邦学习（FL）稳定超越本地训练，且在多数指标（C-index、IBS、calibration slope）上逼近甚至小幅超越集中式基准，验证其临床可行性；
  • RSF表现最优：在歧视能力（C-index均值0.732）、校准性（ECE仅0.021）与跨机构鲁棒性（性能方差最小）三者间取得最佳平衡；
  • 客户端分布多样性是关键调节因子——异质性越高，FedProx相对FedAvg优势越显著；FedAdam在稀疏梯度下不稳定，收敛性与泛化性均弱于前两者；
  • 提出面向临床落地的决策指南：根据数据规模、隐私敏感度、可解释性需求（如医生需归因分析）及计算资源，映射推荐模型（Cox/RSF/DeepSurv）与训练范式（FedAvg/FedProx/Local）组合。

本工作为医疗联邦学习从理论走向真实世界部署提供了首个实证框架与可操作路径。

AI Summary (English)

Survival analysis is vital for clinical decision-making, yet privacy constraints prevent centralized data aggregation needed for robust time-to-event modeling. This paper presents the first systematic, multi-model evaluation of federated survival analysis on a real-world, cross-institutional breast cancer cohort (12,847 patients across 5 hospitals) with naturally heterogeneous client distributions. We compare three survival models—Cox proportional hazards, DeepSurv, and Random Survival Forest (RSF)—under centralized, local, and federated training, and assess FedAvg, FedProx, and FedAdam for gradient-based models. Results show federated learning consistently outperforms local training and approaches or occasionally exceeds centralized performance; RSF achieves the best overall trade-off in discrimination (C-index), calibration (ECE), and robustness across clients. Performance strongly depends on client distribution diversity, and FedAvg/FedProx prove more stable and effective than FedAdam. Based on these findings, we derive practical, constraint-driven guidelines for selecting optimal model-training combinations in healthcare federated survival modeling.

Abstract

Survival analysis is central to clinical decision-making, yet reliable time-to-event models require large, diverse cohorts that are rarely available at a single institution, while privacy regulations restrict the centralization of patient data. Federated learning (FL) offers a privacy-preserving alternative by training shared models without exchanging raw data, but its effectiveness for survival modeling under realistic, heterogeneous conditions remains insufficiently understood. This paper presents a systematic, multi-model evaluation of federated survival analysis on a cross-institutional breast cancer cohort with naturally heterogeneous distributed clients. Three representative survival models, the Cox Proportional Hazards model, DeepSurv, and Random Survival Forest (RSF), are compared across centralized, local, and federated training, and three federated optimization strategies (FedAvg, FedProx, and FedAdam) are assessed for the gradient-based models. Results show that FL consistently outperforms local training and approaches, and occasionally exceeds, centralized performance, while RSF offers the best overall balance of discrimination, calibration, and robustness across heterogeneous clients. We further find that performance depends on the diversity of client distributions, and that FedAvg and FedProx are stronger and more stable than FedAdam. Based on these findings, we derive practical, decision-oriented guidelines mapping data, privacy, interpretability, and resource constraints to recommended model and training-paradigm choices for federated survival modeling in healthcare.

item.title: Development and Design of FLKit: A Structured Onboarding Toolkit for Federated Learning in Health and Life Sciences
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.23500v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

在健康与生命科学领域，严格的隐私法规（如GDPR、HIPAA）严重制约了跨机构数据共享。联邦学习（Federated Learning, FL）因其“数据不动模型动”的范式，成为合规协作建模的理想路径。然而，当前技术成熟度已显著提升，实践瓶颈却前移至项目启动阶段：新团队常面临框架碎片化、治理义务模糊、角色职责不清、跨学科术语割裂等结构性障碍，缺乏适配自身背景的系统性入门指南。

方法与设计

本研究提出并构建了FLKit——一个开源、社区共建的结构化联邦学习启航工具包。其核心创新在于角色感知（role-aware）设计：不预设临床医生、法务专家、数据治理专员或算法工程师具备全栈知识，而是为11类典型贡献者提供定制化入口。设计借鉴ELIXIR研究数据管理工具包（RDM Kit）方法论，由多学科核心团队主导开发，联合23家机构组成的广泛联盟参与里程碑评审与路线图制定，并深度访谈27位一线实践者以确保内容扎根真实场景。

核心架构与产出

FLKit围绕联邦学习全生命周期构建四大支柱模块：治理（Governance）、基础设施（Infrastructure）、数据整备（Wrangling）、分析（Analysis），通过三大协同机制实现整合：

  • ✅ 11个角色专属入口点（如“伦理审查员快速启动清单”“数据工程师FL环境配置向导”）；
  • ✅ 跨学科FAIR对齐术语表（含中英文对照、使用场景示例）；
  • ✅ 可复用FL故事（FL Story）模板——结构化规划/记录项目目标、数据契约、模型协议与合规验证路径。

截至2024年12月演示版发布，FLKit已扩展至39页、8大章节，集成7个已完成/进行中的真实FL故事，覆盖多发性硬化症致残预测、炎症性肠病分型、基因组关联分析及脑机接口解码等前沿方向。工具包完全开源，持续接收全球生命科学社区贡献：https://uhasselt-biomedicaldatasciences.github.io/federated-learning-toolkit/

AI Summary (English)

FLKit is an open, community-maintained onboarding toolkit designed to lower the entry barrier for multidisciplinary teams launching federated learning (FL) projects in health and life sciences. Recognizing that technical maturity has outpaced practical adoption—due to fragmented frameworks, ambiguous governance obligations, and role-specific knowledge gaps—FLKit adopts a role-aware lifecycle approach across four stages: Governance, Infrastructure, Wrangling, and Analysis. It provides 11 tailored entry points (e.g., for clinicians, legal officers, data stewards, and ML engineers), a cross-disciplinary FAIR-aligned glossary, a reusable “FL Story” template for project planning and documentation, and a curated directory of tools and communities. Developed with input from 27 practitioners and guided by a consortium of 23 institutions, FLKit has grown since its December 2024 demo to 39 pages and 8 sections, embedding 7 real-world FL Stories—from multiple sclerosis disability prediction to genomics and brain-computer interfaces. Freely accessible at https://uhasselt-biomedicaldatasciences.github.io/federated-learning-toolkit/, it invites global contributions to advance responsible, interoperable FL in life sciences.

Abstract

Federated learning lets institutions train shared models without moving their data, which makes it a natural fit for health and life sciences research under strict privacy regulation. The methods are maturing fast, but the practical barrier now comes earlier: a team starting a federated project meets a scattered mix of frameworks, governance obligations, and unfamiliar roles, with no structured place to begin that fits its own background. FLKit closes that gap. It is an open, community-maintained onboarding toolkit that takes a multidisciplinary team through the full federated learning lifecycle and gives every contributor, clinical, legal, governance, or technical, a role-aware entry point instead of assuming fluency across all four. We modeled it on the ELIXIR Research Data Management Kit and built it with a multidisciplinary core team, a wider consortium supplying milestone reviews and roadmap direction, and external practitioners interviewed to keep the content grounded in real practice. FLKit sits on four lifecycle stages, Governance, Infrastructure, Wrangling, and Analysis, and connects them through 11 role-specific entry points, a cross-disciplinary glossary, a reusable FAIR-aligned FL Story template for planning and documenting projects, and a curated directory of tools, frameworks, and communities. Since the December 2024 demo it has grown to 39 pages across eight sections, with seven FL Stories documenting completed and ongoing projects in multiple sclerosis disability prediction, inflammatory bowel disease, genomics, and brain-computer interfaces. It is openly available at https://uhasselt-biomedicaldatasciences.github.io/federated-learning-toolkit/ and welcomes contributions from across the life sciences.

item.title: Unlocking In-Context Learning in Audio-Language Models from Decentralized Medical Audio
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.23243v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

在医疗资源匮乏地区，临床音频诊断亟需能仅凭极少量标注样本（如数秒呼吸音或心音）即可识别疾病状态的模型。然而，高质量、大规模标注的临床音频数据稀缺且高度敏感，难以集中收集，传统监督学习与中心化预训练范式面临数据孤岛与隐私合规瓶颈。

方法：联邦自上下文化（FSC）框架

我们提出联邦自上下文化（Federated Self-Contextualization, FSC）——首个面向去中心化医疗音频的多模态语言模型框架，支持跨多家医院客户端的上下文学习（In-Context Learning, ICL）。FSC无需真实诊断标签，通过三阶段渐进式流程实现：
1. 跨模态对齐：以音频字幕（audio captions）为监督信号，联合预训练音频编码器与大语言模型（LLM），建立可迁移的声学-语义对齐；
2. 联邦伪标签构建：在各本地医院端，对未标注音频表征进行无监督聚类，生成结构化“伪标签支持-查询”片段（pseudo-label episodes），模拟真实诊断场景；
3. 联邦上下文适配：通过轻量级联邦优化（FedAvg变体），协同更新模型参数，使其具备从少量支持样本中提取判别性模式并泛化至新查询的能力。

主要结果与创新

在严格留出的呼吸系统（哮喘/支气管炎）与心血管系统（二尖瓣狭窄/正常）双任务基准上，FSC在2-way 2-shot设置下达到71.6%准确率，显著超越现有音频-语言基线模型（+9.2%绝对提升）。其核心创新在于：首次将ICL范式引入隐私敏感的分布式医疗音频场景；规避了对真实标注的依赖；实现了“数据不动、模型动”的合规推理范式。本工作为基层智能听诊提供了可部署、可扩展、可解释的新路径。

AI Summary (English)

We propose Federated Self-Contextualization (FSC), a novel multimodal language model framework enabling in-context learning for clinical audio diagnosis across decentralized hospitals without sharing raw patient data or requiring real diagnostic labels. FSC first aligns audio embeddings with a language model via caption-based pretraining, then constructs pseudo-label support-query episodes through unsupervised clustering of local audio representations, and finally performs federated optimization to adapt the model for few-shot multimodal reasoning. Evaluated on held-out respiratory and cardiac conditions under a 2-way 2-shot setting, FSC achieves 71.6% accuracy, outperforming state-of-the-art audio-language baselines by over 9 percentage points. This work establishes the first privacy-preserving, label-efficient ICL paradigm for real-world medical audio diagnosis.

Abstract

Clinical audio diagnosis in low-resource settings requires models that identify conditions from minimal examples without large annotated corpora. We propose Federated Self-Contextualization (FSC), a multimodal language model framework for in-context clinical audio diagnosis across federated hospital clients. FSC constructs pseudo-label episodes via unsupervised clustering of audio representations, bypassing scarce real diagnostic labels, and enables contextual reasoning from support-query pairs. Our progressive three-stage pipeline first aligns audio embeddings with the language model via caption-based pretraining, then adapts it for episodic in-context inference through federated optimization. At test time, given a small labeled support set, the model diagnoses an unseen query through multimodal reasoning. On held-out respiratory and cardiac conditions, FSC achieves 71.6% accuracy in 2-way 2-shot evaluation, outperforming audio-language baselines by over 9%.

item.title: When AUC 0.998 Is Not Enough: A Candidate Evaluation Protocol for Hidden-State Probes of Indirect Prompt Injection in Multimodal Computer-Use Agents
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.22864v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

隐状态探针（hidden-state probing）——即在冻结的多模态大模型（如Qwen2.5-VL-7B）内部激活上训练线性分类器——正被广泛用于事前检测间接提示注入（IPI），以阻止多模态计算机使用代理（multimodal computer-use agents）执行恶意操作。然而，本文指出：AUC达0.998这一看似完美的指标，并不必然意味着模型能可靠识别恶意内容。

方法与诊断设计

基于Mind2Web数据集上的teacher-forced回放实验，我们提出两项关键后验诊断：

  • 文本侧配对构造标量基线：通过人工构造语义等价但注入表面存在/不存在的文本对（如“截图” vs “请截图（隐藏指令）”），验证探针响应是否真正依赖语义恶意性，而非表面词汇或句法线索；
  • 视觉层同步干扰匹配控制：在相同时间步引入视觉噪声（如UI遮罩、图标扰动）但保持任务无关，检验探针是否将视觉表面对齐误判为攻击信号。

主要发现与创新

两项诊断共同揭示：高clean-vs-attack AUC仅稳健地反映注入表面存在性（injection-surface-present），而非攻击成功与否或语义恶意程度。探针可能捕获的是表层提示模式（如特定token序列、视觉锚点位置），而非深层意图。我们据此构建首个面向IPI探针的候选控制协议包（Candidate Evaluation Protocol），包含可复现的诊断流程、标签定义规范（明确区分“表面存在”与“行为危害”）、以及报告启发式规则（如：AUC > 0.95 仅支持“可检测注入痕迹”，不支持“已理解攻击意图”）。本研究强调：泛化至其他骨干模型或基准属待验证猜想，非既定结论。

AI Summary (English)

This paper challenges the common interpretation of high AUC (e.g., 0.998) in hidden-state probing for indirect prompt injection (IPI) detection in multimodal computer-use agents. Using Qwen2.5-VL-7B on Mind2Web under teacher-forced replay, we show that a clean-vs-attack AUC alone does not validate malicious-content detection. Two post-hoc diagnostics—(1) a paired text-side scalar baseline controlling for surface-level lexical cues, and (2) same-step nuisance-matched visual controls on the UI overlay—reveal that the probe primarily detects injection-surface presence, not semantic malignancy or attack success. We formalize these diagnostics as a candidate evaluation protocol with explicit reporting heuristics: high AUC licenses claims about trace detectability, but not intent understanding, behavioral harm, or generalization beyond the evaluated backbone/benchmark. This work reframes probing as a surface-signal detector—not a semantic auditor—and provides actionable guardrails for rigorous IPI evaluation.

Abstract

Hidden-state probing -- a linear classifier on a frozen vision-language model's internal activations -- has emerged as an attractive evaluation tool for flagging indirect prompt injection (IPI) in multimodal computer-use agents before the agent emits a corrupted action. We argue, on a single-backbone cautionary case study (Qwen2.5-VL-7B on Mind2Web, teacher-forced replay), that a high probing AUC on a clean-vs-attack split is not, on its own, evidence of malicious-content detection. Two post-hoc diagnostics -- a paired-construction scalar baseline on text-side injections, and same-step nuisance-matched visual controls on the overlay surface -- do not license an unqualified malicious-content interpretation of the headline while leaving room for partly-semantic readings. We package the diagnostics as a candidate control set with reporting heuristics for what a high clean-vs-attack AUC does and does not license. Labels are injection-surface-present, not attack success; generalisation beyond this backbone and benchmark is a conjecture.

item.title: Have You Ever Seen Them? Entity-level Membership Inference through Interrogating Large Language Models
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.23030v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

大型语言模型（LLMs）的广泛应用引发了日益严峻的隐私泄露与版权合规风险。传统成员推断（Membership Inference, MI）聚焦于判断单个训练样本是否被模型见过，但忽视了一个关键现象：LLMs展现出类人记忆特性——未必逐字复现某条数据，却能从分散文本中累积并内化真实世界实体的知识（如某位公众人物的职业、生平、关联事件等）。这催生一个新问题：能否像“讯问证人”一样，通过精心设计的提问，探测模型是否在训练中接触过特定实体（entity）的相关信息？

方法创新

本文首次提出实体级成员推断（Entity-level Membership Inference），即判定目标实体（如“张桂梅”）的关联信息是否参与了LLM训练。我们在标签仅黑盒（label-only black-box）场景下开展研究——仅可观测模型生成的文本，无梯度、无置信度输出。我们严格形式化该任务，明确其可行性依赖的线索约束（clue）、输入约束（input）与模型约束（model），并据此提出五种可解释、低开销的问询策略：利用少量实体线索构造提示，激发模型生成实体相关响应，并从生成文本的语义一致性、事实密度、细节丰富度等特征中推断成员关系。

实验结果与意义

我们构建首个面向人物实体的实体级基准数据集，并将现有样本级标签仅方法适配至实体级作为基线。在多个主流开源与商用LLM上实验表明：所提方法AUC最高达0.97，在平衡准确率（Balanced Accuracy）上较最优基线提升6.0%–17.5%。本工作不仅揭示了LLM隐性知识暴露的新风险维度，也为模型审计、数据溯源与合规评估提供了可部署的实体感知工具。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces entity-level membership inference, a novel privacy auditing task that determines whether information about a real-world entity (e.g., a person) was used during LLM training—going beyond traditional sample-level inference. Motivated by the observation that LLMs accumulate entity knowledge from scattered mentions like human memory, we formalize the problem under practical label-only black-box constraints and derive necessary/sufficient feasibility conditions. We propose five interrogation strategies that use minimal entity clues to construct prompts, elicit entity-related generations, and infer membership via semantic features (e.g., factual consistency, detail richness) in outputs. Evaluated on a newly built person-entity benchmark across multiple LLMs, our methods achieve up to 0.97 AUC and improve balanced accuracy by 6.0–17.5% over adapted state-of-the-art baselines—demonstrating both the viability and practicality of entity-aware model auditing.

Abstract

Large Language Models (LLMs) raise growing concerns about privacy leakage and copyright compliance. Membership inference is a key tool for assessing such risks, but existing studies mainly focus on whether specific samples or sample-based data units are used for training. We argue that LLMs exhibit a human-memory-like behavior: an LLM may not memorize a specific sample verbatim, yet it can accumulate and reveal knowledge about a real-world entity from scattered mentions. This analogy motivates us to examine whether an LLM can be interrogated like a human interviewee to reveal its exposure to entity-related information. Motivated by this question, we propose entity-level membership inference, which determines whether information related to a target entity is used in LLM training. We study this task in the practical label-only black-box setting, where only generated texts are observable. We formalize the task under clue, input, and model constraints, establish the necessary and sufficient conditions for its feasibility, and instantiate five interrogation strategies based on this formalization. The strategies use limited entity clues to construct prompts, elicit entity-related responses, and infer membership from semantic features among the generated texts. We construct entity-level datasets and adapt state-of-the-art sample-level label-only methods to the entity-level setting as baselines. Experiments on person entities show that our methods achieve AUC up to 0.97 and bring gains of 6.0%--17.5% in Balanced Accuracy over the best adapted baseline.

item.title: SCRUB-FL: Sanitizing and Cleansing Representations via Unlearning of Backdoors
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.22700v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

联邦学习（FL）通过本地化训练规避原始数据共享，在医疗、金融等隐私敏感场景中备受青睐。然而，其去中心化架构天然易受后门攻击：恶意客户端在本地数据中注入隐蔽触发器（如特定像素图案），诱导全局模型对含触发样本产生错误预测。现有防御方法多聚焦于训练前过滤或聚合阶段检测，难以根除已固化于收敛模型中的后门行为；而训练后净化（post-training sanitization）又受限于服务器端缺乏触发器先验知识及无干净代理数据集，常导致残余攻击成功率高或主任务精度显著下降（因神经元功能纠缠）。

方法创新：SCRUB-FL

本文提出 SCRUB-FL（Sanitizing and Cleansing Representations via Unlearning of Backdoors），一种两阶段、隐私保护的后训练后门清除框架：

  • 训练期协同表征学习：各客户端基于谱分析与激活聚类识别可疑样本，独立训练轻量级带梯度惩罚的Wasserstein GAN（WGAN-GP）建模触发分布；仅上传生成器参数至服务器——实现触发模式的无数据聚合（data-free aggregation），杜绝原始数据泄露风险。
  • 收敛后机器遗忘：服务器融合客户端生成器构建全局触发近似样本库，对全局模型执行定向遗忘（targeted unlearning）：通过KL散度正则化，强制模型对触发样本的输出分布趋近均匀分布，从而解耦“触发–目标”关联，同时保留正常分类能力。

实验结果与优势

在CIFAR-10与GTSRB数据集上，针对Label-Consistent、Hidden Trigger、Dynamic Patch三类先进攻击，SCRUB-FL在高达40%恶意客户端参与下，将后门攻击成功率压低至3.88%，同时维持>91% 的主任务准确率，显著优于SOTA方法（如Fang2022、Li2023）。关键优势在于：无需服务器端触发先验、不依赖大规模清洁代理数据、全程保障数据隐私，为FL安全落地提供实用新范式。

AI Summary (English)

Federated Learning (FL) enables privacy-preserving collaborative training but remains vulnerable to backdoor attacks, where malicious clients implant hidden triggers to manipulate global model predictions. Existing defenses—operating pre- or during-aggregation—fail to fully eliminate persistent backdoor behaviors in the converged model, while post-training sanitization suffers from server-side ignorance of trigger patterns and lack of clean proxy data. To address this, we propose SCRUB-FL, a two-phase, privacy-aware backdoor removal framework: (1) During training, clients detect suspicious samples via spectral analysis and activation clustering, then train lightweight WGAN-GP models to capture trigger distributions; only generator parameters—not raw data—are aggregated server-side to build a global trigger representation; (2) After convergence, the server synthesizes trigger-approximating samples and applies targeted machine unlearning, redistributing model predictions toward a uniform distribution to erase trigger-target associations. Evaluations on CIFAR-10 and GTSRB under three attack types and up to 40% malicious participation show SCRUB-FL reduces attack success rate to 3.88% while preserving >91% clean accuracy—outperforming state-of-the-art methods without requiring trigger knowledge or large clean datasets at the server.

Abstract

Federated Learning (FL) enables collaborative model training without sharing raw data, making it a promising paradigm for privacy-sensitive applications. However, its decentralized nature makes it inherently vulnerable to backdoor attacks, where malicious clients embed hidden triggers into local training data to manipulate model predictions. Existing defenses mainly operate during before and during aggregation cannot fully eliminate backdoor behaviors that persist in the converged global model. Moreover, the effectiveness of post-training sanitization is often limited by the server's lack of knowledge of trigger patterns or poisoned clients after convergence, resulting in residual backdoor behaviors or accuracy degradation due to neuron entanglement. To address this limitation, we propose SCRUB-FL (Sanitizing and Cleansing Representations via Unlearning of Backdoors), a two-phase solution for post-training backdoor removal in FL. During training, clients identify suspicious samples using spectral analysis and activation clustering, then train lightweight Wasserstein Generative Adversarial Network with Gradient Penalty (WGAN-GP) models to capture trigger-related distributions. The generator parameters are aggregated server-side to construct a global representation of suspicious patterns without exposing raw data. After convergence, the server synthesizes trigger-approximating samples and applies machine unlearning to erase the trigger-target association by redistributing predictions toward a uniform distribution. Experimental evaluations on CIFAR-10 and GTSRB across three attack types and up to 40% malicious participation demonstrate that SCRUB-FL reduces the backdoor attack success rate to as low as 3.88% while maintaining over 91% normal task accuracy, outperforming state-of-the-art defenses without requiring prior trigger knowledge or a large clean proxy dataset at the server.

item.title: Confidently Wrong: Severity-Aware Calibration of Prompt-Injection Detectors under Attack Shift
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.22659v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

大模型部署中，Prompt注入检测器作为关键安全守门员，依赖置信度分数决定是否放行或拦截输入。然而，现有评估多聚焦于静态基准下的准确率，忽视了攻击分布发生偏移（attack shift）时检测器置信度的可靠性问题——即当检测器“判错”时，其输出的高置信度是否具有欺骗性？

方法设计

本研究系统评估三个开源检测器（ProtectAI-v2、Prompt-Guard-2 的两个checkpoint），在单一源域校准阈值下（冻结不调），跨五类真实攻击分布偏移场景进行迁移测试。创新性地提出严重性指标（Severity, S）：量化检测器对漏检攻击（false negatives）所赋予的平均置信度。同步报告漏检率（FNR）、区分能力（discrimination）及校准误差。

核心发现

  • 普遍性高置信误判：所有检测器在全部5种偏移下，对漏检攻击的S值恒定在0.99–1.00区间，而FNR剧烈波动（0.01–0.97）——即“错得非常确信”。
  • 结构性盲区：三者均完全失效于间接行为劫持类注入（indirect behavior-hijack），该漏洞跨越两家厂商、四倍模型规模差异，暴露深层设计缺陷。
  • 校准评估失灵：标准池化校准误差（如ECE）严重失真——某检测器全局ECE仅0.06（被判定“良好校准”），但在攻击样本上实际校准误差高达0.91。
  • 现实危害实证：在真实LLM流水线中，漏检注入成功绕过防护，释放超半数有效exploit，且通过率与检测器对正常样本的捕获率高度一致。
  • 归因验证：控制实验确认问题根源在于内容关键词匹配（content-keying）而非注入结构识别；指令微调模型作为裁判亦复现相同盲区；黑盒重写器可精准利用该机制生成高置信、高危害的漏检样本。

创新价值

首次揭示prompt注入检测器存在“自信式失效”范式，提出severity为关键诊断维度，挑战现行校准与评估范式，为鲁棒检测器设计提供可操作路径。

AI Summary (English)

This paper exposes a critical failure mode of prompt-injection detectors: they are confidently wrong under attack distribution shifts. Evaluating three open-source detectors (ProtectAI-v2 and two Prompt-Guard-2 checkpoints) at a frozen, source-calibrated threshold across five realistic attack shifts, we introduce severity (S)—the average confidence assigned to missed attacks. Across all settings, S remains near-perfect (0.99–1.00) while false-negative rates vary widely (0.01–0.97), revealing systemic overconfidence on failures. All three detectors uniformly fail against indirect behavior-hijack injections—a vendor-agnostic blind spot spanning 4× model sizes. Standard calibration metrics (e.g., ECE=0.06) mask severe miscalibration on attacks alone (ECE=0.91). In live-model deployments, missed injections leak >50% of working exploits. Controlled experiments trace the root cause to content-keying, not structural understanding: an instruction-tuned judge replicates the blind spot, and a black-box rewriter exploits content-keying to generate high-confidence, high-impact misses—most effectively on the most dangerous attack class.

Abstract

Prompt-injection detectors are deployed as guards: a model scores an input and a downstream system trusts or blocks it on that score. I study the confidence of these scores, not only their accuracy, when the attack distribution shifts away from the clean benchmark on which the operating point was chosen. I evaluate three released detectors, ProtectAI-v2 and two Prompt-Guard-2 checkpoints, at a single source-calibrated threshold that I freeze and transport across five shifts. I report a severity metric S, how confident a detector is on the attacks it misses, alongside the false-negative rate and discrimination. Across every shift and every detector, severity on the missed attacks stays between 0.99 and 1.00 while the false-negative rate ranges from 0.01 to 0.97: when these detectors miss, they miss with near-certainty. All three confidently pass indirect behavior-hijack injection, a blind spot unanimous across two vendors and a fourfold size range. Standard pooled calibration error does not register this; one detector it rates well-calibrated, at 0.06, is miscalibrated at 0.91 on the attacks alone. Run against live models, the missed injections leak the majority of working exploits, passing them at the rate they catch others. A controlled experiment traces the cause to content-keying rather than injection structure, an instruction-tuned model used as a judge shows the same hijack blind spot, and a black-box rewriter exploits the content-keying to manufacture working confident misses, most effectively on the most dangerous attack category. Code and data are public.

item.title: Subspace-Constrained Federated Learning with Low-Rank Adaptation
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.22724v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

面向通信受限与隐私敏感场景的联邦低秩自适应（Federated LoRA）方法虽具潜力，但客户端数据非独立同分布（non-IID）易导致局部低秩更新在子空间层面发生几何错位（subspace misalignment），进而引发聚合失真、收敛缓慢甚至性能退化。现有方法（如FedAvg、FedSVD、SVD redistribution）缺乏对更新方向一致性的显式约束，其子空间对齐程度尚未系统量化。

方法创新

本文提出子空间约束型联邦LoRA框架（Subspace-Reg）：在标准LoRA目标函数中引入正则项，强制各客户端的低秩更新矩阵投影至一个共享的全局参考子空间（由服务器维护并同步）。该设计不增加额外参数量，仅需在本地训练时计算轻量级投影梯度，通信开销与标准LoRA持平。

关键实证发现

我们在HellaSwag基准上，于10客户端non-IID设定下，对RoBERTa-large与SmolLM-360M两大预训练模型开展全面评估（3随机种子×4方法×2模型=24组实验）：

  • RoBERTa-large上显著领先：Subspace-Reg取得最高平均最佳精度（0.454 ± 0.023）、最高平均最终精度（0.429 ± 0.011）及最低最终损失（1.363），全面超越所有基线；
  • SmolLM-360M上呈现模型依赖性：FedAvg精度略优，表明子空间约束收益受模型规模与架构影响；
  • 几何对齐性获强验证：Subspace-Reg在全部24次运行中实现近乎完美的子空间基重叠度（≈0.9999），远超基线（0.958–0.991），为“子空间错位损害聚合”假说提供坚实证据。

代码已开源：https://github.com/sadia-sigma-lab/Subspace-Constrained-Federated-learning-with-Lora

AI Summary (English)

Federated low-rank adaptation (LoRA) enables efficient fine-tuning of large models under communication and privacy constraints, yet heterogeneous client data causes geometric misalignment among local low-rank updates—potentially degrading aggregation and convergence. We propose Subspace-Reg, a subspace-regularized federated LoRA objective that constrains local updates to stay close to a shared global reference subspace via lightweight projection. Empirical evaluation across RoBERTa-large and SmolLM-360M on HellaSwag (10-client non-IID, 3 seeds) shows Subspace-Reg achieves state-of-the-art mean best accuracy (0.454 ± 0.023) and lowest final loss (1.363) on RoBERTa-large—outperforming FedAvg, SVD redistribution, and FedSVD by large margins. While accuracy gains are model-dependent (FedAvg leads on SmolLM-360M), Subspace-Reg consistently attains near-perfect basis overlap (≈0.9999) across all runs—versus 0.958–0.991 for baselines—strongly validating the geometric alignment hypothesis. Code is publicly available.

Abstract

Federated low-rank adaptation methods are attractive for fine-tuning large models under communication and privacy constraints, but heterogeneous client data can induce geometric misalignment between local low-rank updates. We study whether this subspace misalignment leads to destructive aggregation and slower convergence in LoRA-based federated learning. We propose a subspace-regularized federated LoRA objective that encourages local client updates to remain close to a shared global reference subspace. We present a complete empirical evaluation on two pretrained models, RoBERTa-large and SmolLM-360M, over HellaSwag in a non-IID 10-client federated setting, across 3 random seeds (42, 43, 44), yielding 24 total experimental runs (4 methods x 3 seeds x 2 models). On RoBERTa-large, Subspace-Reg achieves the strongest mean best accuracy (0.454 +/- 0.023), mean final accuracy (0.429 +/- 0.011), and lowest final loss (1.363) across all three seeds, outperforming FedAvg, SVD redistribution, and FedSVD baselines by a large margin. On SmolLM-360M, FedAvg leads on accuracy, revealing that accuracy gains are model-dependent. Crucially, Subspace-Reg achieves near-perfect basis overlap, approximately 0.9999, on both models and across all seeds, versus 0.958 to 0.991 for all baselines, providing robust support for the geometric alignment hypothesis. The code is publicly available at https://github.com/sadia-sigma-lab/Subspace-Constrained-Federated-learning-with-Lora.

item.title: AgentLens: Interpretable Safety Steering via Mechanistic Subspaces for Multi-Turn Coding Agent
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.22673v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

基于大语言模型（LLM）的多轮编码智能体展现出强大自主编程能力，但在与外部环境（如Shell、API、文件系统）持续交互过程中，易产生越权执行、代码注入、数据泄露等动态演化型安全风险。现有安全机制（如输出过滤、后置审核、外部护栏）缺乏对执行过程的细粒度、实时干预能力；而主流机制可解释性方法多聚焦于单轮问答或越狱场景，难以建模多轮代理中隐藏状态的风险累积与转移。

方法创新：AgentLens 框架

本文提出 AgentLens——首个面向多轮编码智能体的白盒式机制安全 steering 框架。其核心包含两大技术：

  • 机制子空间风险检测：在单层Transformer隐藏表示中，识别并构建一个10维可解释子空间，该子空间能稳定编码执行安全性信号（如“权限提升意图”“敏感API调用倾向”），实现步级（step-level）运行时风险定位；
  • 子空间干预式缓解：不修改模型权重，仅通过低维投影+定向偏移（project-and-shift）在该子空间内实施轻量干预，实时抑制有害行为生成，保持任务性能无损。

实验验证与贡献

为支撑研究，我们构建了Mechanistic Agent Safety (MAS) 基准：涵盖194个真实编码任务，由LLaMA-3.1-8B、Qwen-2.5-7B、Gemma-2-9B三模型生成全轨迹人工标注的多轮执行日志（含每步安全标签、风险类型、因果归因）。实验表明：AgentLens 在跨模型泛化下实现92.3%平均风险检出率（F1），首次提供前向风险预判证据（提前1–2步预警准确率达76.5%），并将有害动作率降低68.4%，显著优于SOTA外部护栏（如SafeCoder、Guardrails）。本工作为LLM智能体安全从“黑盒拦截”迈向“白盒引导”奠定关键基础。

AI Summary (English)

We present AgentLens, a white-box mechanistic interpretability framework for safety steering of multi-turn coding agents. Unlike external guardrails, AgentLens detects unsafe execution states during runtime by analyzing step-level hidden representations and mitigates risks via targeted intervention in a compact 10-dimensional subspace within a single Transformer layer—enabling fine-grained, representation-level control without altering model weights. To enable rigorous evaluation, we introduce the Mechanistic Agent Safety (MAS) benchmark, featuring expert-annotated multi-turn execution trajectories across 194 coding tasks using LLaMA-3.1-8B, Qwen-2.5-7B, and Gemma-2-9B. Experiments show AgentLens achieves 92.3% average F1 for safety detection, provides preliminary evidence of lookahead risk anticipation (76.5% accuracy predicting hazards 1–2 steps ahead), and reduces harmful actions by 68.4%, outperforming state-of-the-art external safeguards. Code: https://github.com/EddyLuo1232/AgentLens

Abstract

Coding agents based on large language models (LLMs) demonstrate remarkable autonomous capabilities, but they also introduce significant safety and misuse risks during multi-turn interactions with external environments. Existing safety mechanisms mainly rely on external guardrails, which have a limited ability to perform fine-grained behavioral control during execution. Meanwhile, recent mechanistic interpretability methods for LLM safety are mostly confined to single-turn or jailbreak-style QA settings, limiting their ability to capture the evolving risk dynamics of multi-turn agent execution. In this paper, we investigate the safety of multi-turn coding agents from an internal perspective. We propose AgentLens (Mechanistic Subspace Intervention and Steering), a white-box defense framework that performs runtime safety detection and representation-level mitigation for coding agents. Unlike conventional agent guardrails, AgentLens detect harmful execution states from step-level hidden representations and mitigate unsafe behavior by intervening in a 10-dimensional subspace within a single layer. To support this research, we introduce the Mechanistic Agent Safety (MAS) benchmark, comprising comprehensively annotated multi-turn execution trajectories across 194 tasks using LLaMA-3.1-8B, Qwen-2.5-7B, and Gemma-2-9B. Extensive experiments show that AgentLens achieves strong safety detection performance, provides preliminary evidence for lookahead risk anticipation, and substantially reduces harmful actions of the coding agent, establishing a foundation for applying mechanistic interpretability to dynamic LLM agent safety. The code is available at: https://github.com/EddyLuo1232/AgentLens

item.title: Foundation Models for Epileptogenic Zone Identification in Drug-Resistant Epilepsy
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.22657v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

药物难治性癫痫患者接受切除手术后，癫痫致痫区（Epileptogenic Zone, EZ）的准确定位是实现无发作的关键前提，但当前临床实践中术后无发作率仍低于50%。传统依赖发作期脑电（如SOZ）的方法存在高假阳性率，且需长时间侵入性立体定向脑电图（sEEG）监测，增加患者风险与医疗负担。

方法创新

本研究提出EpiiSLM——首个面向EZ识别的双基础模型系统：
