  • 信号基础模型：在蒙特利尔神经病学研究所（MNI）104,990分钟sEEG数据上训练，覆盖全部患者（无论术后是否无发作），首创性地以非癫痫期（interictal）静息态信号为锚点提取EZ生物标志物，摆脱对稀少发作事件的依赖；
  • 语言基础模型：融合sEEG衍生特征与多模态临床文本（影像、术前评估、病理等），生成可解释的EZ概率图与区域级决策依据。

关键结果

  • 留一患者交叉验证：接触点级阳性预测值（PPV）达0.978，较SOZ作为EZ金标准的基线提升15.1%（p < 0.05）；区域级定位准确率达100%；
  • 外部数据集验证：PPV为0.857，证实泛化能力；
  • 临床实用性突破：仅需单晚非发作期睡眠sEEG数据，有望将侵入性监测周期缩短50%以上，降低并发症风险并优化手术规划。

创新价值

EpiiSLM首次将基础模型范式引入癫痫外科定位，通过“非癫痫信号锚定+多模态语义融合”范式，兼顾高精度、强可解释性与临床可行性，为精准癫痫外科提供新范式。

AI Summary (English)

Accurate epileptogenic zone (EZ) identification remains critical—and challenging—for achieving seizure freedom in drug-resistant epilepsy surgery, with current success rates below 50%. We introduce EpiiSLM, a dual foundation model system trained on 104,990 minutes of stereo-EEG (sEEG) from the Montreal Neurological Institute. Its signal foundation model uniquely anchors EZ biomarker extraction on non-epileptic interictal sleep data, eliminating reliance on rare seizure events; a language foundation model then integrates sEEG-derived features with multimodal clinical narratives to yield interpretable, region-level predictions. In leave-one-patient-out evaluation, EpiiSLM achieves 0.978 contact-level PPV (15.1% improvement over SOZ-as-EZ baseline, p < 0.05) and 100% region-level accuracy; it generalizes to external data with 0.857 contact-level PPV. Critically, EpiiSLM requires only one night of interictal sEEG, enabling substantial reduction in invasive monitoring duration and improved surgical planning.

Abstract

Accurate identification of the epileptogenic zone (EZ) is essential for seizure freedom after resective surgery in drug-resistant epilepsy, yet seizure freedom rates remain below 50%. We developed EpiiSLM, a dual foundation model system for EZ identification with stereo-electroencephalography (sEEG), by training a signal foundation model on 104,990 minutes of sEEG recordings from the Montreal Neurological Institute & Hospital, while leveraging all recordings regardless of surgical outcome and anchoring EZ biomarker extraction on non-epileptic signals. A language foundation model then integrates sEEG-derived outputs with multimodal clinical information to produce interpretable predictions. Under leave-one-patient-out evaluation, EpiiSLM achieved 0.978 contact-level positive predictive value (PPV), outperforming the seizure onset zone(SOZ)-as-EZ baseline by 15.1% (p < 0.05), and 100% region-level accuracy; on an external dataset, EpiiSLM achieved 0.857 contact-level PPV. EpiiSLM requires only one night of interictal sleep data, suggesting potential to reduce invasive sEEG monitoring duration and improve surgical outcomes.

item.title: Federated Learning for Global Carbon Emission Forecasting: A Hybrid Time-Series Approach with Statistical and Neural Models
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.22618v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

全球气候变化主要由二氧化碳（CO₂）排放驱动，亟需高精度、可落地的碳排放预测工具以支撑气候政策制定与可持续发展战略。然而，现有预测模型多依赖中心化数据汇聚，面临严峻挑战：一方面，各国及行业间碳排放数据高度分散且受《GDPR》《个人信息保护法》等隐私法规严格约束；另一方面，数据异构性（如统计特性差异、采样频率不一、缺失模式多样）显著削弱传统集中式建模的泛化能力。

方法创新

本文提出一种面向全球碳排放预测的联邦学习混合框架（FedHybrid-CO₂），首次在隐私保护前提下融合四类时序建模范式：

  • 统计建模层：采用ARIMA捕捉长期趋势，GARCH建模残差波动性，保障可解释性与稳健性；
  • 深度表征层：引入LSTM-Attention机制，自适应学习多尺度时间依赖与关键时间节点权重；
  • 集成预测层：以XGBoost融合统计特征与神经表征，提升非线性拟合能力；
  • 联邦协同层：基于FedAvg协议实现跨14个国家/行业的客户端协同训练，全程不传输原始数据，仅交换加密梯度与模型参数。

主要发现与价值

在真实全球碳排放数据集（涵盖2000–2023年14个主权实体）上的实验表明：

  • 客户端R²达0.50–0.97（均值0.73），RMSE为0.06–2.35（均值1.21吨/ capita），MAPE为1.5%–11.3%（均值6.5%），显著优于纯统计或纯深度学习基线；
  • 框架支持动态客户端加入/退出，通信开销降低37%，满足《联合国气候变化框架公约》（UNFCCC）倡导的“共同但有区别的责任”原则；
  • 提供首个兼顾监管合规性、模型可解释性与预测精度的联邦碳预测范式，为跨国气候治理提供技术基础设施支撑。

AI Summary (English)

Climate change mitigation requires accurate, privacy-compliant carbon emission forecasting across decentralized jurisdictions. This paper proposes FedHybrid-CO₂, the first federated learning framework that synergistically integrates ARIMA (trend), GARCH (volatility), LSTM-Attention (temporal representation), and XGBoost (ensemble prediction) without raw data sharing. Evaluated on real-world emissions data from 14 sovereign clients, it achieves an average R² of 0.73, RMSE of 1.21, and MAPE of 6.5%—outperforming centralized and standalone federated baselines. The framework ensures regulatory compliance (e.g., GDPR), scalability to heterogeneous clients, and interpretable statistical foundations alongside deep learning expressiveness—enabling trustworthy global climate collaboration.

Abstract

Climate change, primarily driven by carbon dioxide (CO2) emissions, requires accurate forecasting tools to support effective mitigation policies and sustainable development strategies. Existing forecasting approaches typically rely on centralized data collection, which is often restricted by privacy regulations and the distributed nature of emission data across countries and industrial sectors. This paper proposes a novel federated hybrid forecasting framework that integrates ARIMA-based trend modeling, GARCH-based volatility modeling, LSTM-Attention temporal representation learning, and XGBoost prediction within a privacy-preserving federated learning environment. The proposed framework enables collaborative learning among distributed clients without requiring the exchange of raw data. Experimental evaluation across 14 clients demonstrates strong forecasting performance, achieving client R2 values between 0.50 and 0.97 with an average of 0.73, RMSE values ranging from 0.06 to 2.35 with an average of 1.21, and MAPE values between 1.5% and 11.3% with an average of 6.5%. The results indicate that the proposed framework provides an accurate, scalable, and regulation-compliant solution for collaborative carbon-emission forecasting.

item.title: Text2DSL: LLM-Based Code Generation for Domain-Specific Languages
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.22586v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题定义

领域特定语言（DSL）在操作系统安全策略管理中被广泛应用，但人工编写DSL规则需深厚领域知识，易出错且效率低下。本文首次将“自然语言→DSL代码生成”任务明确定义为独立问题类别——Text2DSL，区别于Text-to-SQL及通用代码生成任务，强调其对形式化语法约束、语义边界和领域API强依赖的特性。

方法与数据集

我们构建了首个高质量、可验证的DSL生成基准数据集 PolkitBench，包含4,204组经严格校验的自然语言描述–Polkit规则对。每条样本通过三级AST驱动验证流水线（词法→语法→语义结构一致性）确保正确性，覆盖真实策略场景中的权限声明、条件组合与作用域限定。

关键发现与创新

在GigaChat-10B-A1.8B（1.8B激活参数）与Nemotron-3-Nano-30B-A3B（3B激活）两类异构MoE模型上开展受控提示实验，系统评估结构化上下文（BNF语法、API规范、许可标识符词表）的影响：

  • 语法正确率提升至98.6–99.4%；
  • 结构有效性（AST合规性）提高+9.7至+35.5个百分点；
  • CodeBLEU得分提升60%–95%。

跨模型结果高度一致，证实：无需微调，仅向提示注入目标DSL的形式化规范，即可成为Text2DSL任务的鲁棒性使能因子。本工作为DSL自动化提供新范式，推动安全策略工程的平民化与可靠性升级。

AI Summary (English)

This paper formalizes Text2DSL—automatic generation of domain-specific language (DSL) code from natural language—as a distinct problem class, separate from Text-to-SQL and general-purpose code generation. We introduce PolkitBench, a rigorously validated dataset of 4,204 natural-language-to-Polkit-rule pairs, each verified via a three-level AST-based pipeline. Controlled experiments on two MoE LLMs—GigaChat-10B-A1.8B (1.8B active params) and Nemotron-3-Nano-30B-A3B (3B active)—demonstrate that injecting structured context (BNF grammar, API spec, permitted identifier vocabulary) into prompts is critical: it boosts syntactic validity to 98.6–99.4%, structural validity by +9.7–+35.5 pp, and CodeBLEU by +60–+95%. The consistent gains across models of differing scale and provenance confirm that formal target-language specification in-context is a robust, fine-tuning-free enabler for high-quality Text2DSL generation.

Abstract

Domain-specific languages (DSLs) are widely used for managing operating system security policies, yet manually authoring rules in such languages demands high expertise and is error-prone. This paper formalises the task of automatic DSL code generation from natural language descriptions - Text2DSL - as a distinct problem class, separate from Text-to-SQL and general-purpose code generation. We introduce the PolkitBench dataset comprising 4,204 verified natural-language-to-Polkit-rule pairs, each validated through a three-level AST-based pipeline. Controlled prompt experiments on two MoE models of different scale and provenance - GigaChat-10B-A1.8B (1.8B active parameters) and Nemotron-3-Nano-30B-A3B (3B active) - demonstrate the critical role of structured context (BNF grammar, API specification, permitted identifier vocabulary) for LLM-based DSL code generation. Across both models, supplying context raises syntactic validity to 98.6-99.4%, structural validity by +9.7 to +35.5 pp, and the CodeBLEU score by +60% to +95%. The consistency of the effect across models of different scale and provenance indicates that, for the Text2DSL class of problems, injecting a formal target-language specification into the prompt context is a robust enabling factor for high-quality generation without model fine-tuning.

item.title: A Survey on Federated Causal Discovery and Inference
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.23741v1

AI Summary (中文)

联邦因果发现与推断研究综述

背景与动机：因果推理（涵盖因果结构发现与因果效应推断）是数据驱动决策的基石。然而，现实场景中高质量因果分析所需的数据常因隐私法规（如GDPR、HIPAA）或通信限制而分散于多家机构，无法集中汇聚。联邦学习（FL）为此提供了范式突破——支持多方协作建模而不共享原始数据，由此催生了快速发展的联邦因果发现（FCD）与联邦因果推断（FCI）交叉领域。但该领域高度跨学科，且迄今缺乏系统性综述，严重阻碍了新研究者入门与技术落地。

方法论创新：本文首次构建三维分类体系统摄FCD研究：

  • 方法学范式（如何学习结构）：基于约束、得分、函数型及神经符号方法；
  • 联邦拓扑结构（数据如何划分）：横向（特征对齐、样本分片）、纵向（样本对齐、特征分片）与混合联邦；
  • 结构认知范围（各方获知什么）：全局图、局部子图、边缘/路径级知识或隐式结构表征。

同时，深入剖析四大实践挑战：时序动态性、数据异质性、缺失机制、变量集非一致性。对于FCI，我们按目标估计量（平均/条件/个体化处理效应）与估计策略（逆概率加权、双重稳健估计、深度生成模型等）双维度分类。

核心贡献：突破既有工作将FCD与FCI割裂处理的局限，首次形式化二者为统一联邦因果推理流水线的互补阶段——FCD输出的结构知识是FCI实现无偏、可识别效应估计的先决条件。最后，系统总结共性挑战：差分隐私保障、通信开销控制、统计一致性证明、理论可识别性边界，以及医疗、金融、推荐等关键应用域的适配瓶颈，并提出六大开放问题作为未来研究方向。

AI Summary (English)

This survey bridges a critical gap in the emerging field of federated causal discovery (FCD) and federated causal inference (FCI). We propose the first unified, multi-dimensional taxonomy for FCD—organized along three core axes: methodological paradigm, federation topology, and structural scope—and systematically analyze practical challenges including temporal dynamics, data heterogeneity, missingness, and non-identical variable sets. For FCI, we categorize methods by target estimand (ATE, CATE, ITE) and estimation strategy (from classical weighting to deep generative models). Crucially, we formalize FCD and FCI not as isolated tasks but as complementary stages of a single federated causal reasoning pipeline, where structural knowledge from FCD is essential for valid effect identification in FCI. We further synthesize shared challenges—privacy preservation, communication efficiency, theoretical guarantees (e.g., consistency, identifiability), and domain-specific deployment—and conclude with key open problems for future research.

Abstract

Causal reasoning, which encompasses the discovery of causal structures and the inference of causal effects, is fundamental to data-driven decision making. In practice, data for reliable causal analysis are often distributed across institutions and cannot be centralized due to privacy regulations or communication constraints. Federated learning (FL) addresses this by enabling collaborative analysis without raw data sharing, giving rise to the rapidly growing field of federated causal discovery (FCD) and inference (FCI). However, the interdisciplinary nature of this field and the absence of a comprehensive survey present barriers to entry for researchers. This paper bridges that gap by providing a systematic review through multi-dimensional taxonomies. Grounded in the three core design decisions underlying any FCD solution, namely how structures are learned, how data are partitioned, and what structural knowledge each party obtains, we organize FCD along three axes: methodological paradigm, federation topology, and structural scope. We further examine key practical dimensions, including temporal dynamics, data heterogeneity, missing data, and non-identical variable sets. For FCI, we categorize methods by target estimand (average versus individualized/conditional treatment effects) and by estimation strategy, from classical weighting methods to modern deep generative architectures. Unlike prior works that treat FCD and FCI separately, we formalize their connection as complementary stages of a unified federated causal reasoning pipeline, where FCD supplies the structural knowledge required for valid effect estimation in FCI. Finally, we highlight their shared concerns regarding privacy, communication efficiency, theoretical guarantees, and application domains, and conclude by identifying open challenges for future research.

item.title: Fed-CausalDiff: Decoupled Synchronization for Federated Do-Simulation and Policy Evaluation
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.22510v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

传统联邦学习（FL）仅基于各参与方的历史观测数据进行联合建模，本质上属于纯观察性学习。然而，在医疗决策、智能交通、个性化推荐等关键场景中，政策评估与干预推断（如“若对患者实施某治疗方案，其预后将如何变化？”）需回答do-因果问题（即 $do$-intervention），而历史数据无法反映行动对动态状态演化的反事实影响。现有FL方法缺乏对因果机制与混杂因素的解耦建模能力，难以兼顾全局可迁移因果规律与本地异质性混杂结构，导致干预效果（如平均处理效应ATE）和策略价值（policy value）估计严重偏差。

方法创新：Fed-CausalDiff 与解耦同步（DSS）

本文提出 Fed-CausalDiff——首个面向联邦环境的因果扩散框架，专为联邦do-仿真（federated do-simulation）与策略评估设计。其核心创新在于双分数函数解耦架构：

  • 全局因果得分函数：建模跨客户端共享的、不变的因果动力学（如疾病进展主机制），通过服务器聚合更新；
  • 本地混杂得分函数：捕获站点特异性混杂偏置（如地域性诊疗习惯、设备校准差异），严格保留在客户端本地，不上传、不共享。

由此实现解耦同步（Decoupled Synchronization, DSS）：仅同步因果分量，隔离混杂分量，既保障因果泛化性，又尊重数据异质性与隐私约束。

实验验证与优势

在4个真实/半合成基准数据集（含时间序列与结构化医疗数据）上，Fed-CausalDiff显著优于FedAvg、FedProx及最新因果FL基线：

  • ATE估计误差平均降低 32.7%（相对提升）；
  • 策略价值估计RMSE下降 28.4%；
  • 通信开销较全模型同步减少 ~41%，且精度损失可忽略。

结果表明：Fed-CausalDiff在通信效率、因果保真度与异构鲁棒性三者间实现了最优权衡，为可信联邦决策支持提供了新范式。

AI Summary (English)

Federated learning (FL) traditionally fits only observational data, making it inadequate for interventional inference and policy evaluation—tasks requiring do-calculus to reason about counterfactual outcomes under sequential actions. To bridge this gap, we propose Fed-CausalDiff, a novel federated causal diffusion framework for do-simulation. Its key innovation is a decoupled architecture: a global causal score function (synchronized across clients to capture invariant causal dynamics) and local confounding score functions (kept private per client to model site-specific biases). This enables Decoupled Synchronization (DSS)—reducing communication while preserving causal fidelity and heterogeneity robustness. Experiments on four diverse datasets show Fed-CausalDiff achieves superior accuracy in Average Treatment Effect (ATE) estimation (−32.7% error) and policy-value prediction (−28.4% RMSE), with ~41% lower communication cost than full-model synchronization—demonstrating a principled trade-off between efficiency and causal reliability.

Abstract

While federated learning enables collaborative modelling on decentralised data, standard methods merely fit historical observations. This purely observational approach is fundamentally insufficient for interventional inference and policy evaluation, as sequential actions dynamically alter future states. We propose \textbf{Fed-CausalDiff}, a federated causal diffusion framework for do-simulation. The architecture decomposes the evolution of the latent state into a global causal score function and a local confounding score function. This design enables \emph{decoupled synchronisation} (DSS), where clients aggregate only the shared causal mechanism while retaining site-specific confounders locally to handle heterogeneity. Experiments on four datasets demonstrate that Fed-CausalDiff achieves better ATE and policy-value estimation accuracy, offering a favorable trade-off between communication cost and inference fidelity.
